Name: 1/3 of Healthcare Organizations Paid Ransom to Restore Their Data
Introduction Paragraph: Ransomware is a serious threat to healthcare organizations. Find out how it works, why it targets healthcare, and how to prevent and recover from an attack.
Post Body:
People depend on hospitals, clinics, medical offices and other healthcare providers to be there when they need them the most. In turn, healthcare organizations depend on technology to be able to provide care. It is an industry where 24-hour availability of services is, quite literally, a life or death matter. For healthcare organizations, IT downtime is not only unacceptable but potentially threatening to human lives. Perhaps this is why 34% of healthcare organizations attacked by ransomware in the past year admitted to paying the ransom to try to get their data back and minimize disruption to services.

Sophos, a leading cybersecurity company and AllSafe IT partner, published a report on The State of Ransomware in Healthcare 2021. The survey of 328 IT decision makers across globe revealed that 34% of organizations surveyed were hit by ransomware in the past year. Of those, 65% said that the cybercriminals were able to encrypt their data, making it unavailable and essentially useless. As mentioned above, 34% – over a third – of these paid a ransom to recover their data. Again, part of this is because of the urgency to get their systems back so they could continue service.

But alarmingly, another reason that healthcare organizations elected to pay up is because they were less prepared to restore from backup. The report showed that only 44% were able to use backups to restore their data, compared to the global average of 57% for other industries. Of all industries surveyed, healthcare ranked the second lowest in their ability to restore from backup.

As far as costs, the average ransomware payment was $131,304. However, paying the ransom is a poor strategy. Healthcare organizations reported that they only received an average of 69% of their data back after paying up. The remaining third of their data stayed encrypted and therefore inaccessible. Across industries, only 8% said that they got all their data back after paying ransom.

But the ransom itself is only a small fraction of the total cost required to recover from a ransomware attack. When figuring the total cost, including downtime, loss of business, device and network cost, etc, healthcare organizations spent an average of $1.27 million in recovery costs after a ransomware attack.

Why is the Healthcare Industry So Vulnerable?

The Health Sector Cybersecurity Coordination Center (HC3) is an entity created by the United States Department of Health and Human Services to identify and communicate cybersecurity risks with the healthcare and public health (HPH) sector. So far, HC3 has tracked 82 ransomware incidents impacting healthcare in 2021 (as of May 25). This includes the attack on the Irish healthcare system, which forced major disruption and cancellation of non-critical services. Of the 82 ransomware attacks logged in 2021, 48 were in the United States and 10 were in California.

One reason for the uptick in cases is that healthcare organizations don’t tend to prioritize their spending on IT security. In fact, hospitals are notorious for investing only 1/10 the amount spent by other industries on technology and cybersecurity. As a result, their IT systems tend to be outdated and end-of-life, lacking the latest security patches.

Healthcare records contain sensitive personal data, including patients’ identification info, medical histories, prescriptions, diagnoses, financial info, etc. This makes them extremely valuable to scammers and identity thieves.

These factors, combined with the inherently urgent nature of healthcare services, make the healthcare industry an attractive and easy target for cybercriminals.

What can Healthcare Organizations do to prevent or mitigate an attack?

AllSafe IT recommends the following best practices:

  • Deploy a multi-layered stack of security solutions like AllSafe IT’s Safe Total, which includes endpoint protection, advanced email security, multi-factor authentication, security awareness training and compromised credential monitoring.
  • Implement Backup and Disaster Recovery (BDR). Restoring from backup is the easiest, cheapest and most reliable way to restore data after an attack.
  • Make sure you’re HIPAA compliant. According to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, being HIPAA compliant can help prevent ransomware or help organizations recover from ransomware.
  • Assume you will be hit. We only hear about huge corporations in the headlines, but healthcare providers of all sizes are targeted. Do everything you can to avoid an attack, but also be prepared for the worst.
  • Have a malware recovery plan. Speaking of preparing for the worst, make sure you have an incident response plan in place. This will enable healthcare IT teams to quickly mobilize when a ransomware attack starts and may even be able to stop an attack before data can be encrypted.
  • Don’t pay the ransom. Organizations that pay ransom only have an 8% chance of getting all their data back. This is a very poor return on investment indeed.

AllSafe IT is proud to have spent over 15 years providing IT services to hundreds of companies—many of those within the healthcare industry. Our specialized services are uniquely tailored to healthcare IT support to provide our customers with the reliability, protection, and fast services needed to ensure 100% uptime, maximized data security, and importantly, resources for comprehensive HIPAA compliance. From comprehensive and preventative cybersecurity strategies to 24/7 customer support to multi-layered HIPAA compliance, AllSafe IT understands the robust—and unique—needs of healthcare businesses.

Name: 10 Best IT Professional Services You Need For Your Business
Introduction Paragraph: In order to truly understand why businesses need IT professional services companies, it is important to understand the types of professional IT services.
Post Body:
What Are Professional Services in IT?

Whether big or small, all businesses and companies are dependent on IT professional service companies since technology is the backbone for all businesses. It may be easy to assume that IT services refer to a single set of services, but in reality, IT services is an umbrella term made up of diverse groups of services.

From network management to cybersecurity, data management, and even set up and installation, top professional firms in the world offer a wide array of services for their clients.

Many options are available for IT professional services; therefore, businesses have to shortlist the professional services they need and find a service provider accordingly.

10 Main IT Service Categories:

To truly understand why businesses need IT professional services, it is essential to review the different types available. Here are the ten main categories of IT services that are popular and in-demand.

1. Managed IT Services:

Specific sets of needs are the same for all businesses and companies irrespective of their niche or area of services. This list of IT professional services is known as managed IT services. Managed IT services can include setting up the network infrastructure, managing, installing, maintaining equipment, and even IT troubleshooting.

Many businesses may believe that these responsibilities are not that tiring and that their company can handle them internally. In reality, the load of these services can increase and become a burden over time.

Therefore, it is best to delegate these tasks to a third-party service provider who can take on the responsibility and ensure that the equipment runs smoothly without any issues. Businesses often turn to managed IT services in Los Angeles for reliable, full-scale support, while companies in Pasadena and Orange County benefit from localized solutions tailored to their specific operational demands.

2. On-Demand IT:

As the business grows, so do its IT needs. There comes a point where the company develops specific needs and requirements that an IT professional services provider can best address. The list of these custom IT-related services is what is known as on-demand IT services.

From consulting and advisory services to the development of special products and services specific to the business's need, these services require a level of expertise and field experience to identify potential gaps and make recommendations that are best suited to your specific industry.

3. Network Setup:

No one can deny the significance and importance of a stable internet connection. It is an integral part of our day to day lives, but it is even more critical for businesses. Therefore, having a proper network setup should be a non-negotiable.

The right IT professional service provider will take the time to complete a site audit. They will ensure that they identify any possible pain points and suggest the proper hardware to ensure that your wired and wireless connections are fast and reliable.

4. Network Security:

Businesses have started to realize the significance of protecting their data and related resources. Cybersecurity and network security have evolved as emerging fields that are now one of the most popular professional IT services in the market.

From the evaluation of the security mechanism, testing, and even proposing solutions to avoid and mitigate any threat, network security is an extensive IT service that is most certainly important and one that all business owners cannot ignore at any cost.

5. Database Management:

Data is one of the most important resources for any business, whether enterprise-scale or small. Data should be managed, stored, and secured accordingly so that business owners can utilize it properly.

These functions are all referred to as database management. There are plenty of IT consulting companies that will gladly take this responsibility off your plate.

Database management is even more critical for businesses that take a data-driven approach and interact, collect, and deal with data regularly.

6. Cloud Computing:

Rather than relying on physical storage options and wired connections, businesses are now moving towards the cloud computing approach. It allows companies to store their data on a cloud platform, which is secure and makes the data more accessible.

Cloud computing is amongst the list of services provided by IT companies that are extremely valuable, and almost all businesses are now demanding this service.

Companies can sign up for a shared cloud service or have a designated cloud storage option depending on their needs and requirements.

7. Help Desk Services:

Printers, scanners, and computers, etc., are present in every office. This equipment needs to be set up and installed. The configuration of these devices is exactly where the help desk IT services can come in.

The repairs, setup, and maintenance of IT-related equipment is the responsibility of these service providers, ensuring that these items keep running smoothly.

8. Data Backup:

Is your data safe? Data loss is a risk that businesses cannot ignore. The operational and financial repercussions of lost data can amount to thousands of dollars, not to mention real setbacks in business processes.

The only way to mitigate this possible problem is by enlisting help from IT consulting service providers and investing in data backup services. These solutions give companies the peace of mind they need to operate their day-to-day business and ensure that all of their data is easily accessible should they face any on-premise issues.

9. IT Consulting:

Businesses always need to have an IT consultant on board to assess the company and develop IT-based solutions. IT consulting is a top-rated IT service that enables businesses to make the right choices and identify business opportunities.

It is always useful to have an expert on hand that makes suggestions based on years of experience and can foresight to anticipate your businesses' needs.

10. Social Media Consulting:

The modern era is the age of social media. Advertisements, marketing, connections, and social media serve as channels to deliver all these services under one roof.

Businesses need to have a social media consultant on board to ensure that they use the power of their social channels correctly. Social media consultants design social media strategies to maintain an excellent social media presence and interact with current customers and try and lure in potential ones.

How To Find A Professional Service Provider?

By now, you are aware of the top ten IT services in demand and are most certainly a requirement for every business, whether big or small. But the main question remains unanswered. How do you find a professional service company that is truly reliable and dependable?

When you begin your search to find the top-rated professional service firms in the market, you will come across a handful of options, but beware, not all companies can live up to their claims of excellence.

IT is such an integral component for all businesses. It is essential to properly vet your potential partner, ensuring that they are the right fit for your company and, most importantly, making sure there is a compatibility between your business and their offerings.

Research is Important!

We cannot stress the importance of research enough. You will find tons of firms that tout their managed IT services and promise you the world, but you need to take time and vet each company.

Do not blindly trust the first IT service provider that you come across. Make sure you ask the right questions.

Have they worked with a business in your vertical before?

Do they understand your pain points?

Is communication with their team easy and enjoyable?

Do not under any means, let your decision come down to cost. Just because their rate is more affordable doesn't mean they are the best fit for you!

Keep in mind this is a partnership, so you want a company that understands your vision and will do all it can to help you reach your goals. Lastly, remember nothing beats experience.

Many service providers in the market will claim to be the best of the best. But what is the real measure that can help you find the right IT service provider? Experience.

Whenever you find a service provider, make sure that your prospect can provide references and real-life case studies of previous work they have completed.

Conclusion:

When looking for a professional IT service provider, you need to be vigilant and careful. Before going on a hunt to search for an IT service provider, make sure that you know the different IT professional services. After realizing the types, you can assess your business's needs and finalize which services are integral. Then you can move ahead and find a service provider that can take care of the responsibility and provide high-quality IT services for your business.

Name: 10 Solutions to Speed Up your Slow Computer Systems in 2023
Introduction Paragraph: Is your computer running slow? Learn how to speed up your PC with these simple tips and tricks from AllSafe IT. Boost your performance today!
Post Body:
We’ve all heard the old adage that “time is money.” And this is especially true in the business world. In today’s business climate, with technology making everything available at one’s fingertips, things move at a much faster pace. As a result, a slow computer can be a huge issue for employees if it causes them to miss urgent deadlines and fall behind in productivity. The ripple effect of this can affect an entire company, forcing them to pay workers for extended hours with less resulting work product, potentially losing dissatisfied customers, and ultimately impacting the company’s bottom line.  

If you have had the misfortune of dealing with a slow computer, you are well aware of how frustrating it can be. Whether the slowdown occurs at startup, when trying to browse the Internet or use web apps, or when attempting to work with specific applications, slow and unresponsive computers are a pain to work with.

Luckily, we are here to help! First, we’ll provide some insight and reasons why your computer may not be performing as fast as it could be. Then we’ll share our five best tips on how to fix the issues that are causing the slowdown. Keep reading.  

Reasons Your Computer Is Slow:

Before diving into some of the things that you can do to speed up your PC, it is important to be aware of some of the common reasons why your computer might be slow. The biggest mistake that people make which in turn leaves them worrying about their computer slowing down is not shutting down the computer properly. When you put the laptop or computer to sleep rather than switching it off, it does not flush out the programs from the memory of the computer. Rather, it temporarily pauses the computer. This can take a toll on the RAM and lead to your computer to slowing down.

Another common reason that might answer the question, “what is slowing my computer down?” is the installation of  too many programs or applications on your computer. Applications and programs take up space in the memory and thus often end up slowing down the computer’s processor. This causes lags causing your computer to delay even when performing the simplest of tasks.

Installing heavier programs like games and video and image editing software can also take up too much space on the computer and end up slowing down the computer. If you have to use such programs, it is recommended that you consider upgrading the computer memory so that your computer is prepared to handle the load of such programs without slowing down.

Lastly, more often than not, your computer might be slowing down because of malware. Malware runs in the background undetected. it can silently exhaust the computer’s resources and end up causing overall lag and delays.

Related Article : Managed IT Services Los Angeles

How To Make Computer Faster?

Here are some tried and tested effective tips that will help you solve this issue ultimately improving CPU performance.

1. Limit Programs That Launch At Startup:

You might have noticed that some programs are launched as soon as you switch your computer on. These are the programs that the computer automatically launches at startup. Some of these programs are the programs that are actually needed, but there are plenty of others that are useless.

The programs that are automatically launched start running in the background and thus take up the CPU resources. Applications that you are not even using end up exhausting your system ultimately these programs are slowing down your PC.

Go to your computer’s settings and change them to adjust the programs that are launched at startup. To do so:

  • Open your task manager. You can do so by going to Settings, opening Apps and then clicking the Startup Tab.
  • You will see a list of programs with a toggle option that allows users to disable or enable the programs that will be launched at startup.
  • Go through this list and only enable the programs that you require immediately after starting your computer and disable the rest. This will boost your computer’s speed and you will notice a significant difference in its performance.

2. Uninstall Unnecessary Programs:

We often find ourselves downloading too many apps and programs on our computers. We may think we might need or will use them, but 9/10 times that is not the case. These apps and programs take up space on our computers and occupy memory. This in turn leads to complaints about our windows 10 running slow.

If you want to effectively speed up your computer, go through the list of the apps and programs that you have installed on your computer and only keep the ones that are absolutely essential and necessary. To do so, you can follow these steps:

  • Click the start button.
  • Go to the settings of your computer.
  • Go to the Apps tab and click it.
  • You will see a list of all the programs and applications that are installed on your computer.
  • Browse to the program that you wish to uninstall, click on it and then press the uninstall button.
  • The app that you selected will be removed from your system.
  • Now you can go ahead and repeat the same process for the other applications that you wish to remove.

3. Perform Windows Disk Cleanup:

If you are unsure about which programs and applications are contributing to slowing down your system, you should perform the windows disk cleanup. It is an automatic disk cleanup strategy. Windows runs the disk cleaner which automatically detects the files and programs that have not been used in a while. Then it will ask for the user’s permission and if granted, the files detected will be removed from the system.

Some people are intimidated by the automatic disk cleanup process because they think it will remove files without asking them first. But in reality, the users are shown the list of the detected programs as well as the space that the program has occupied. Then you can choose the programs that you wish to remove and keep the ones that are essential.

4. Consider Upgrading The Memory:

One of the major reasons that contribute to the slowing down of any computer system is too much pressure on memory. If you only have the programs and applications that are absolutely necessary installed on your system, you should consider upgrading your computer memory.

Some people think that they can upgrade their computer’s memory only by changing their laptops or computers. But in reality, you can easily get the memory upgraded while keeping the same machine.

Upgrading the memory is a great choice for people like programmers, video and image editors, and gamers. These programs and applications are generally heavier than common applications and require more space and may end up taking a toll on your computer’s memory and leading to a slow computer. You should either invest in computers that are specifically developed for these tasks or upgrade the memory of your current computer to fulfill these needs.

5. Scan Your Computer For Malware:

At times we can be careless about the safety and security of our computers. This oversight can leave our computers prone to malware and unauthorized hacking attempts. Lagging can be a simple indicator of this type of breach.

If you are sick of a slow laptop and are wondering about how to improve computer performance, you should run a complete scan of your laptop to be certain that your laptop is free from any sort of malware. Make sure that you have an anti-virus installed on your computer and that you run scans on a regular basis. Moreover, avoid downloading fishy email attachments or browsing websites and internet channels that are not secure. The use of a VPN is also recommended as it will protect your online identity and conceal your online presence thus providing a secure fortress around your computer and reducing the risk of a malware attack.

In Conclusion

We hope this article has given you the tools necessary to identify the reason why your system may be running slow. Most importantly, you now have the general know-how and the tools to care for your system. Remember, you do not need to feel frustrated with constant lags and delays, your computer should serve to increase your productivity and relieve headaches not cause them.

Name: 13 Essential IT Services for Business Success
Introduction Paragraph: Explore the 10 most impactful IT services, including cloud solutions, cybersecurity, and managed IT services, tailored to support and secure your business.
Post Body:
Updated: November 30, 2025

Explore the 13 most impactful IT services, including cloud solutions, cybersecurity, and managed IT services, tailored to support and secure your business.

Running a business today is no small feat. Every decision, every delay, every hiccup in your systems impacts your bottom line.  

You don’t need more stress. You need solutions that work.  

Let me paint you a picture: Your data is backed up, your network is secure, and your employees have access to reliable support services anytime they face technical issues. That’s the peace of mind that the right IT services can bring.

However, not all IT solutions are created equal.  

Modern businesses need comprehensive IT services to stay competitive and operate efficiently. The right combination of IT services and solutions can transform your operations while protecting critical assets.

Whether you're dealing with outdated software, struggling with cybersecurity threats, or wondering if it’s time to outsource some responsibilities, understanding the types of IT services available to you is the first step toward running your business like a well-oiled machine.

Summary

  • Business IT services include cloud solutions, cybersecurity, managed IT services, and support services
  • IT solutions for companies range from basic help desk support to complete infrastructure management
  • Understanding types of IT services helps you make informed technology investment decisions
  • Professional IT and business services provide specialized expertise without internal staffing costs
  • Strategic selection of business IT services delivers measurable ROI and operational improvements

What are IT services, and why do they matter?

IT services encompass a range of solutions designed to keep your business running smoothly. From managing data and securing your network to ensuring employees have access to tools that streamline workflows, these services are essential for modern businesses of any size.

Think of it this way: technology is the backbone of your business operations. Without proper network management or reliable data backup, your business risks losing valuable, sensitive information or experiencing crippling downtime.

Here’s why they matter:

  • They provide solutions to address common challenges like cybersecurity breaches and system failures.
  • They enhance business continuity by minimizing disruptions caused by technical issues.
  • They allow companies to focus on core business functions instead of juggling IT concerns.

When you choose services tailored to your business needs, you don’t just avoid problems—you actively create opportunities to optimize your processes, protect your assets, and grow your business.

The top 13 types of IT services explained

Understanding the types of IT services available is critical to choosing the ones that will help your business thrive. Below, we break down the most impactful services and solutions that modern companies rely on to stay competitive and secure.

Managed IT services

Managed IT services are the backbone of most IT setups. A managed service provider (MSP) handles everything from monitoring your systems to providing regular maintenance. These services help desk support, troubleshooting, and proactive problem-solving to keep your operations running seamlessly.

Cybersecurity services

Protecting your sensitive information from breaches is non-negotiable. Managed security services include network monitoring, threat detection, and data encryption, ensuring your business is secure against evolving cyber threats.

Cloud computing services

Cloud solutions are changing how businesses operate. They provide platforms to store and access information, run applications, and scale operations with flexibility. Whether it’s software as a service (SaaS) or complete cloud service infrastructure, this option is indispensable for businesses looking to innovate.

Data backup and recovery

The loss of critical data can devastate any business. With reliable backup services, you can protect and recover essential information should any issues occur. These services often include automated backups, ensuring business continuity.

VoIP services

Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) enables efficient communication systems without the need for traditional phone lines. This service allows employees to stay connected, whether working remotely or in-office and integrates seamlessly with other IT tools.

IT help desk support

When your team encounters technical glitches, immediate assistance is essential. Help desk support ensures employees can resolve issues quickly, reducing downtime and frustration.

Network management services

A stable network is the foundation of reliable IT operations. This includes monitoring, optimization, and troubleshooting to keep systems running smoothly and minimize risks.

Cloud solutions and management

Beyond storing data, cloud services provide businesses with tools to enhance productivity, enable remote work, and facilitate seamless collaboration across teams. They also include robust data center services for secure, scalable storage.

Business continuity and disaster recovery planning

Business continuity and disaster recovery planning focuses on preparing your business for unexpected disruptions. It combines data backup, system redundancy, and recovery protocols to minimize downtime and keep your business operational during emergencies.

Application and software management

From installation to regular updates, managing devices and software ensures your team has access to the latest technology without compatibility issues. This service can also include technology training for business teams to maximize efficiency.

IT asset management

IT asset management tracks and optimizes every piece of technology in your business, from software licenses to hardware warranties. This service helps you avoid unnecessary purchases, ensure compliance, and plan for future technology needs.

Compliance and regulatory support

Compliance and regulatory support helps businesses navigate complex requirements like HIPAA, Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX), and California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA). These services include policy development, staff training, and ongoing monitoring to ensure your technology practices meet industry standards.

Digital transformation consulting

Digital transformation consulting helps businesses modernize their operations through strategic technology adoption. This service evaluates your current processes, identifies automation opportunities, and creates implementation roadmaps that improve efficiency without disrupting daily operations.

What are business consulting services in IT?

IT business consulting helps bridge the gap between your technology and business goals. When your business needs strategic guidance, consultants analyze your current setup, spot improvement opportunities, and create roadmaps that align tech investments with growth plans.

We've seen many Southern California businesses make costly technology mistakes during rapid expansion. IT consultants bring industry expertise to help you avoid these pitfalls and accelerate digital transformation.

For growing companies, consulting services provide objective assessments of your technology stack. Consultants evaluate whether your current systems can support expansion. They recommend how to align your business with IT and upgrade systems to deliver measurable ROI.

Understanding business connectivity services

Business connectivity services keep your teams, locations, and applications talking to each other seamlessly. These services include network design, internet connectivity, VPN setup, and integration between cloud and on-premises systems.  

This is important because strong connectivity forms the foundation for remote work, multi-location operations, and cloud adoption.

Modern businesses need multiple connectivity layers. Your primary internet connection supports day-to-day operations. Backup connections prevent costly outages that could shut down productivity. VPN services secure remote access, while SD-WAN technology optimizes traffic between locations.

Business support services: 4-tier IT support

Business support services cover the full spectrum of IT help your company needs to operate efficiently. These services range from basic help desk support to complex infrastructure management. They handle everything from password resets to disaster recovery planning.

Support services work on tiered levels.  

Level 1 support handles routine issues like software installations and user account management.  

Level 2 support tackles more complex problems that require specialized knowledge.  

Level 3 support addresses critical infrastructure issues and advanced troubleshooting that keeps your business running.  

Level 4 involves vendor escalation or external specialist consultation for issues beyond internal expertise. This level ensures you get manufacturer support and specialized technical resources when needed.

We've found that most LA-area professional services firms need a mix of all support levels. They want quick resolution for everyday issues but also need expert-level support when systems face serious problems.

Need reliable IT support that scales with your business? Explore our managed IT services designed for growing companies.

How to choose the best IT services for your business

Finding the right IT services for your small business isn’t just about what’s trendy—it’s about solving problems specific to your company's needs. Here’s a step-by-step approach to ensure you select services that deliver measurable results.

Assess your business needs

Start by identifying your biggest pain points. Are you struggling with network security? Do you need better data backup options? Or is your team losing time due to constant technical issues? Defining these needs will help you determine which services tailored to your business can make the most impact.

Consider scalability

As your business grows, so will your IT demands. Look for solutions like cloud service options or managed IT services that can adapt to your evolving business processes without requiring constant overhauls.

Evaluate the provider’s expertise

Not all providers are created equal. Choose a managed service provider with a proven track record, offering services and solutions that align with your industry’s specific requirements. A reliable provider offers support, like help desk services, along with proactive monitoring and maintenance.

Prioritize security

In today’s environment, cybersecurity should be at the forefront of any IT strategy. Look for providers that emphasize managed security services to protect your sensitive information and ensure business continuity.

Look at service integration

The best services include seamless integration with your existing tools and workflows. Whether it’s setting up cloud computing, managing devices and software, or offering remote support, your IT services should feel like a natural extension of your business operations.

Ask about ongoing support

Your provider’s services team should offer round-the-clock technical support, ensuring minimal downtime and fast resolutions to any issues.

Choose a local provider

A local provider offers the added benefit of on-site support and a deeper understanding of your business needs. This can be especially helpful in industries with specific compliance requirements or those that handle sensitive data. You can try searching "IT services near me" or "managed service provider near me" to find local options.

When to outsource IT services

Deciding whether to handle IT in-house or outsource can be a tough call. However, for many business owners, outsourcing to a trusted managed service provider (MSP) is the most practical choice. Here’s how to know if outsourcing is right for you:

Your team is overwhelmed

If your internal team is struggling to keep up with IT demands, it’s a sign you may need external help. An MSP can take over critical tasks like network management, cybersecurity, and cloud computing, allowing your team to focus on their core responsibilities.

Downtime is costing you

Every moment of downtime impacts your bottom line. Whether it’s due to system failures or cybersecurity breaches, outsourcing ensures you have a dedicated services team monitoring and maintaining your infrastructure 24/7.

You lack the specialized expertise

The IT landscape is constantly evolving. From data backup to security services, outsourcing gives you access to specialists who understand the latest technology and can manage these services for organizations like yours.

You want cost-effective solutions

Hiring and training in-house IT staff can be expensive, especially for small businesses. Outsourcing allows you to tap into top-tier expertise without the overhead, giving you access to services tailored to your needs at a fraction of the cost.

You’re ready to grow

As your business scales, so do its IT requirements. Outsourcing ensures you have flexible solutions, such as cloud services and managed services, that can grow with your business.

Final thoughts

Running a successful business today means having reliable IT services that address your unique challenges and prepare you for the future. Whether you’re a small business aiming to scale or a larger organization looking to streamline your business processes, the right solutions can save you time, money, and stress.

Outsourcing to an experienced managed service provider (MSP) ensures you gain access to cutting-edge technology, robust cybersecurity, and scalable options like cloud service platforms. Name: 4 Lessons To Learn From Delta’s DR Failure
Introduction Paragraph: Delta lost millions of dollars in damages an IT outage. Does your company have a disaster recovery plan? Learn how you can be prepared with our IT Support.
Post Body:
Delta is paying big for the IT outage that occurred last month: millions of dollars in damages, 2300 cancelled flights, and significant reputational damage. Despite the harsh cut to the airline’s bottom line, Delta will probably still survive.

But the real question is this: Can your business survive after long periods of downtime? A natural disaster, power outage, or successful hack can be the downfall of many small- to medium-sized businesses. But if you learn from the lessons of Delta’s IT mishap, your organization has a good chance of staying on its feet.

Strive for 100% redundancy

According to Delta’s chief information officer, a power failure caused the company’s data center to crash, grounding thousands of would-be passengers. Although power was restored six hours after the incident, critical systems and network equipment failed to switch to a secondary site, corrupting valuable data in the process. And while some systems failed over, other vital applications didn’t; this created bottlenecks, decreased revenue and diminished customers’ confidence

Delta’s case is a massive wake up call not just for the airline industry but for every business - large and small. Companies must implement disaster recovery plans for their data centers, on-site technology, and Cloud applications to continue servicing customers while fixing the main issue with their primary systems.

Companies also need to get rid of the false notion that redundancy plans to assure service continuity is restricted to larger corporations. DR and business continuity solutions are extremely affordable today, and a partnership with a provider can help you in more ways than one (more on this later).

Always test your backups

So although Delta had a plan to bring its business back to normalcy, the DR plan left a lot to be desired in practice. This begs the question as to whether the airline company is actually testing, reviewing, and reinforcing its vulnerabilities to different disasters.
The point is that even though your company may have a failover protocol in place, that protocol adds no value to your business unless it has been rigorously tried and tested. In order to avoid the same fate as Delta, make sure to find out whether your disaster recovery plan is capable of running mission-critical applications like email and customer service applications before - not after - downtime occurs.

Account for different types of vulnerability

In an interview with the Associated Press, Delta CEO Ed Bastian said, “We did not believe, by any means, that we had this type of vulnerability.” Indeed, it’s often hard to foresee what threats and vulnerabilities a natural disaster, power outage, or hacker can produce.But it’s not impossible.

By conducting a comprehensive audit of your data center security and disaster protocols, your business will be more aware and adept at minimizing the risk of potential disasters. This also means evaluating and preparing for disasters that are likely to happen to your business depending on its geographic location. Southern US, for instance, is prone to hurricanes and flooding.

‍Call for help

‍These lessons and strategies are all crucially important, but pulling off a DR and business continuity solution on your own may be difficult. For this reason, it’s critical to have a planned partnership with a managed services provider that can assess, plan, test and install the continuity solutions your business needs in order to minimize the impact and avoid encountering a Delta IT outage of your own
To find out more about business continuity and guaranteeing complete IT redundancy, contact us today.

Why Use A VPN?

Before diving in deep and sharing the list of the top five best free VPN for Windows 10, it is important to shed light on the significance of using a VPN. From torrenting to sharing files and resources and even for protecting their online browsing history, people have been using vpns for quite some time. But is it something that is truly necessary? Here are some of the advantages associated with the use of a VPN:

1. Online Security:

When we are browsing online, we have to share personal details like name, banking information, address and other sensitive information on various portals. If we do so without the protective shield of a VPN, we risk exposing this sensitive information. This data can be manipulated and can even pose a threat to our safety. However, when we use a VPN, this information is concealed and thus cannot be used to harm us in any way.

2. Accessing Block Sites:

Some sites block users based on their geographical locations. If you want to cross such restrictions, using a best free VPN for Windows 7 is your best bet. Since a VPN hides the location from which the user is accessing the website, therefore, it can be used to access the sites that are otherwise blocked due to the user’s geographical location.

3. Protecting Online Browsing History:

Many people do not realize this, but their online browsing history can be used to track them and keep an eye on their online presence and activities. How Does A VPN Work?

VPN stands for Virtual Private Network. When you do not use a VPN, the computer’s IP address is used to send the webpage request to the webserver. Therefore, it is easy for anyone to track your computer’s IPS and use it to identify you.

However, when you use a VPN, your computer’s IP address is rerouted and it goes to the VPN’s server. Here a cover-up IP address is assigned to the request and the required web page is fetched. This way, the website you access cannot see your computer’s actual IP address thus protecting your online identity completely. Through the use of cloud based storage options, you get the freedom to store as much data as needed. Therefore, running out of storage space is not something you will need to worry about.

Name: 5 Key Security Considerations For Protecting Remote Workers
Introduction Paragraph: This article comprises five work from home cyber security tips so that both the employees as well as the company can be safeguarded and protected
Post Body:
CoronaVirus pandemic has forced companies to adapt remote working practices. It is important to ensure that companies are aware of considerations for remote workers so that the risk of security issues with working remotely can be avoided. This article comprises five work from home cyber security tips so that both the employees as well as the company can be safeguarded and protected. IT services for remote workers are a great resource that will help employees and make working from home easier and at the same time, more efficient for them. Making use of the right softwares, ensuring access control for the company data and resources and practicing small things that ensure employees can be safeguarded are effective ways of ensuring that the company targets are being met, employees are working and everything is going on smoothly despite the global pandemic and the consequences it has brought about all over the world.

The CoronaVirus pandemic has taken the entire world by storm. There is nothing that could have prepared the world to be able to deal with this situation beforehand. As the situation got out of hand and the number of infected cases started to exponential growth, companies were forced to switch to remote working.

What could possibly go wrong when workers work from their homes? Is there a need to spread remote working security awareness? What are some of the security issues with working remotely? These are some questions that companies need to address given the current situation. This article will cover all the information about support and security considerations to safeguard remote workers in a comprehensive manner so you can ensure that your company has adopted the best technology for working remotely.

Role Of IT Support For Remote Workers

What Is IT Support?

IT Support is an umbrella term that encompasses a diverse range of IT services. IT Support can be as simple as providing networking and internet facilities for your company to ensuring that the company data is protected and access control is deployed.

Speaking in terms of remote working practices, there are plenty of IT Services that companies can make use of. There are many softwares that make remote working easier and more efficient. There are some employee monitoring softwares through which employers can keep an eye on the contributions and work of their employees, time management softwares as well as security protocols that protect both the companies as well as the employees.

The virtual meeting rooms and collaboration platforms are also an example of IT Supports for remote workers. Through these tools, the company employees and employers can collaborate and stay in touch with one another even if they are not working and meeting in offices.

Need Of IT Support For Remote Workers:

Before we dive in and discuss tips for safe remote working, it is important to discuss why IT considerations for remote workers are important. When working in an office it is very easy for the employers and the employees to coordinate and communicate with another. However, this is not the case when workers are working on a remote basis.

Remote working does not eliminate the need for communication and collaboration. Therefore, it is important for companies to make use of IT services for remote workers that virtually bridge the gap between employees and employers and their colleagues.

Other than communication and collaboration platforms, another challenge that companies may face when switching to remote working practices is safeguarding information. Whether it is the personal information and data of the employees or the sensitive information and data of the company itself. Making sure that all the information remains safe and does not go in wrong hands is extremely important. IT supports for remote workers in this regard are therefore a necessity.

Benefits Of IT Support:

IT Support takes away a lot of load and pressure from the shoulders of the employees. In case of remote working, IT Support makes sure that the employees and employers remain connected. Whether it is holding virtual, online meetings or using platforms to work together, it can be rightfully stated that IT Support makes working on a remote basis a lot easier.

Other than offering support for employers and employees, work from home security solutions are also offered by IT consulting for remote workers. In a nutshell, IT support and services ensure that the company and employers can operate safely and without any hassle. The use of IT Support not only safeguards the company but also protects the employees.

Security Considerations To Safeguard Remote Workers:

In order to make sure that the company can operate properly and work effectively even when workers are working from home, there are some considerations for remote workers that the company owners and employers should be aware of.

If you as a company or employer want to make sure that the challenges associated with remote working platforms can be overcome, here are five key security considerations that will protect your employers and ensure that everything goes on smoothly.

1. Using Official IDs And Antivirus Tools:

As mentioned earlier, there is a diverse range and set of IT Support softwares that are especially developed for remote workers. Many of these tools are also used when working in physical offices.

But since now the situation is completely different and almost all companies have now switched to remote working, it is important to sign up for these IT Support softwares even if they were not being used before.

One of the things that should be the top priority of employers is to ensure that their employees' information remains safe. To be able to do so, strictly set a policy that the employees will only make use of their official email addresses to communicate with each other.

Other than this, remote workers' best practices demand that all employees remain responsible and make use of an antivirus tool. A lot of communication takes place through emails when workers are working from home. Emails may be a great source of communication but at the same time, they are the most common source of virus transmissions.

Therefore, the use of an antivirus tool is necessary so that not only the employees personal computer can remain safe but the entire office and the people he is connected with also remain safe.

2. Access To Company Resources:

Many times, companies provide laptops and other resources for their employees. When workers are working from offices, they don’t need to ask for access. But since now the workers have switched to remote working practices, therefore, the company will need to revise the company access policies.

Access control is very important, therefore, make sure that you invest in a reputable and trustworthy IT Service provider. Only the relevant people and employees should be able to access the company data. Logs should be maintained so records can be maintained about who accessed which data and what changes were made.

Another tip to ensure safety is to make sure that employees make use of their official ids when accessing company data. Multiple authentication techniques can also help make sure that only relevant personnel can access the company data.

Two factor authentication is a great authentication technique that is foolproof and secure. Plus it can be incorporated into the company portals and websites with ease. The workers will have access to all the company resources they need but at the same time, the access will be authenticated so unknown people cannot view or use the data.

3. Revising Firewall Policies:

Many companies make use of firewalls to make sure that the company resources, sensitive information and data remains in safe hands. The company laptops and computers are granted access to these resources without any issue.

But since most of the workers will now be using their personal computers and laptops, the company will need to revise their firewall policies. Ask the employees to make a list of their IP addresses so these addresses can be given a pass to move through the firewall.

You might also experience an increase in the traffic of your website as now the employees will be logging in to the official portals and visiting the company websites multiple times a day. Therefore, this might be the right time to increase the traffic bandwidth for the website so it does not fail to perform or bear the load.

4. Adopt Helpful Softwares:

There are many companies and employees who have never worked from home before. Therefore, they are unaware of softwares that can help them work in an efficient manner. As an employer, it is important for you to keep an eye on the performance of your employees to make sure that they are working properly and the company targets are being met.

Employee monitoring softwares is a great way to do so. These softwares allow employers to see how long the employees worked, assign tasks, provide feedback for the work and also record their screen activity and get a record of the websites they are accessing.

These softwares also motivate the employees to work as they allow proper time management and the employees are compelled to do their work properly since they know they are being monitored.

Using collaborative platforms like Google Drive and Google Docs and Sheets etc. is another great way of collaborating when all the workers are not meeting in a physical setting. Everyone can pool in and add their share of the work and the data can be accessed in real time.

Skype, Zoom and Hangout Meet are great virtual meeting rooms through which the employees and employers can meet when working from home. They offer features like screen sharing and recording of the meeting as well. Microsoft Teams is another great collaborative platform that can be used in a professional setting.

5. Educate And Support Your Employees:

As mentioned earlier, there is a mass majority of people who have never worked in a remote setting before. Having to switch from a physical office to moving towards remote work and that too given the current stressful and anxious COVID-19 situation.

This is a tough time for everyone. Therefore, employers and company owners should be empathetic towards their employees. Communicate with your employees and tell them that they have your support. Call them and check in on them to make sure they are doing well.

It is also an undeniable fact that not all employees are tech savvy and comfortable switching to remote working practices. Therefore, the company will have to offer training and teach the employees how to adapt to this change.

Conclusion:

The current situation may be tough, but as they say, the work must go on. Offices are not operating like they used to and almost all companies have now moved to remote working practices. It is the need of the hour to not only focus on getting work done but also ensure that the employees are protected and safeguarded. IT supports for remote workers is a great way of being certain that the employees are safe.

Make sure YOU never land on this countdown

Fortunately, there are some steps you can take to protect your business from ransomware attacks and recover from them if they happen. In this blog post, we will share some tips and best practices from the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), CISA, and other experts on how to prevent and respond to ransomware attacks.

Prevention Tips

The best way to avoid ransomware attacks is to prevent them from happening in the first place. Here are some prevention tips that you can implement in your business:

  • Use antivirus software at all times. Make sure your antivirus software is set up to automatically scan your emails and removable media (such as flash drives) for ransomware and other malware.
  • Keep all computers fully patched with security updates. Run scheduled checks to keep your operating systems, applications, and firmware up-to-date with the latest security patches.
  • Use security products or services that block access to known ransomware sites on the internet. These products or services can prevent your computers from connecting to malicious domains or IP addresses that host ransomware or other malware.
  • Configure operating systems or use third-party software to allow only authorized applications to run on your computers. This can prevent ransomware from working by restricting the execution of unauthorized or unknown programs.
  • Restrict or prohibit use of personally owned devices on your organization’s networks and for telework or remote access. Unless you are taking extra steps to ensure security, such as using a virtual private network (VPN) or a secure cloud service, do not allow employees to use their personal devices (such as laptops, tablets, or smartphones) to access your organization’s data or systems.
  • Use standard user accounts instead of accounts with administrative privileges whenever possible. This can limit the damage that ransomware can do by reducing the access rights of the infected user account.
  • Avoid using personal applications and websites, such as email, chat, and social media, on work computers. These applications and websites can expose your computers to phishing emails, malicious links, or infected attachments that can deliver ransomware or other malware.
  • Avoid opening files, clicking on links, or downloading attachments from unknown sources. Before opening any file, link, or attachment, check them for suspicious content. For example, you can run an antivirus scan on a file, or inspect a link carefully by hovering over it with your mouse cursor.

Response Tips

Despite your best efforts, you may still fall victim to a ransomware attack. If that happens, you need to act quickly and effectively to minimize the impact and restore your operations. Here are some response tips that you can follow in case of a ransomware attack:

  • Develop and implement an incident recovery plan with defined roles and strategies for decision making. Having a plan in place can help you respond faster and more efficiently to a ransomware attack. Your plan should include steps such as isolating the infected devices, notifying the relevant stakeholders, contacting law enforcement, assessing the damage, restoring backups, and recovering data.
  • Carefully plan, implement, and test a data backup and restoration strategy. One of the most effective ways to recover from a ransomware attack is to restore your data from secure backups. You should backup your data regularly and separately from your main network, using offline storage devices or cloud services. You should also test your backups periodically to ensure that they are working properly.
  • Maintain an up-to-date list of internal and external contacts for ransomware attacks. You should have a list of people or organizations that you can contact in case of a ransomware attack, such as IT staff, management, legal counsel, public relations, customers, vendors, law enforcement agencies, cybersecurity experts, and information sharing groups.
  • Do not pay the ransom. Paying the ransom is not recommended for several reasons. First, there is no guarantee that you will get your data back or that it will not be corrupted or compromised. Second, paying the ransom may encourage more ransomware attacks in the future. Third, paying the ransom may violate laws or regulations that prohibit transactions with certain entities or countries.

Conclusion

Ransomware attacks are a serious threat to your business, but they can be prevented and mitigated with proper planning and preparation. By following the tips and best practices outlined in this blog post, you can protect your data, systems, and reputation from ransomware attacks. If you need more information or assistance on ransomware prevention and response for your business, contact us today. Stay safe and secure!

Name: 5 Ways to manage and Motivate Your Remote team
Introduction Paragraph: Remote work is here to stay. Find out how you can keep your remote team engaged and motivated with these five proven tips from Allsafe IT.
Post Body:
The Covid-19 pandemic has taken the entire world by storm. Companies and businesses all around the world are suffering. One of the challenges companies are facing is how to manage and motivate a remote workforce. Employees are also finding it difficult to work as a remote team and are seen struggling with their productivity levels and getting work done when they have to switch from working from offices to working from home. There are several ideas for remote team building and remote team management strategies that can be deployed in this regard. You can work to improve the motivation of your employees and workers when they are working from home. However, given the current situation, make sure that you are empathetic towards your team and give them some space. It is a tough situation that the world is facing these days and companies should set rightful expectations from their workers and employees.

The COVID-19 pandemic has affected the entire world. It has been a year since the virus made its first appearance but unfortunately, there has been no success in the efforts of devising a vaccine for the virus. Due to the rise in the number of cases on a regular basis and the severity of the virus attacks, schools and offices have been instructed to shut down. As a result, every company has now been forced to opt for remote working practices.

Shifting from working from office to managing remote employees has not been a smooth transition for most companies. The biggest challenge that companies had to face was ensuring productivity even when the employees were working in the form of a remote team.

Reasons Behind Demotivation:

Almost all companies reported that their employees were facing trouble concentrating and staying motivated to work. Queries like how to keep team motivated working from home and how to keep your team motivated remotely were being put forward by companies from all around the world. The companies are already going through a financial crisis given the situation. Therefore, losing more assets due to demotivated employees is not a risk any company can afford to take at the moment.

Before diving into the different effective ways and strategies through which you can learn how to motivate employees during lockdown and ensure that the company is able to achieve its goals, it is important to shed light on why the employees have been feeling this way.

The Covid situation is full of uncertainty. Things are changing at a rapid rate and unfortunately, most of the changes are contributing to making things even worse than before. Given this time, most of us feel anxious. Our lifestyles have changed drastically and we have started to practice unusual things like social distancing and wearing a mask etc. while calling them as the ‘new normal’. Therefore, it is natural for your employees to also feel under pressure and anxious. Bear in mind that their mental health would also be going through a lot that will surely have an adverse effect on their productivity and the motivation to work.

Moreover, all companies have now been forced to switch and now will be managing work from home employees. This has disrupted the normal routine. Before the virus struck, everyone had a proper schedule and routine. Waking up in the morning, getting ready and then going to the office. But now everyone has been confined to their homes. This change has surely affected employees' motivation to work and companies have reported a decrease in their worker’s productivity.

Motivating Employees During Remote Working Practices:

Now that you are aware of the reasons which have led to a decrease in the employees’ productivity and motivation during this lockdown situation, let’s go over five powerful, efficient, tried and tested practices that are the answer to the most asked question i.e. how to manage and motivate a remote workforce.

1. Prioritize Tasks:

A company is always working on multiple projects and tasks at the same time. But given the tough situation and decrease in the motivation of the workers, it is important that the companies prioritize the tasks.

Rather than assigning the tasks in bigger chunks and asking the employees to do all the tasks at once, it is advised that you assign the tasks in small lists. When breaking down the tasks, prioritize the items that need to be done urgently and ask the workers to accomplish those tasks first. Then you can move on to the other tasks.

Breaking the task down and dividing the work into smaller portions is a strategy that will prove beneficial and helpful for both the company as well as the workers. Prioritizing tasks would allow companies to keep track of all the work that needs to be done. Dividing tasks into simpler tasks would make it easy for them to be completed on target.

At the same time, the employees will not feel overburdened when the tasks are assigned in bits and pieces. They will be able to work on smaller tasks with ease rather than feeling that the company is expecting too much from them.

2. Ensure Regular Communication:

Communication is a very important element that every company has to be vigilant about. Lack of communication is the key reason behind eighty percent problems that companies face and come across.

Now that everyone is working from their homes and companies are worried about how to motivate employees working from home, it is important to stress upon the significance of communication. Although you might not be able to meet your workers physically, but make sure that you utilize the virtual means of communication. Hold meetings on a regular basis. Other than work related meetings, feel free to use emails, messages and calls to check up on your employees to ask them about their health and wellbeing.

This is a tough situation that the world is facing right now. People are losing hope and anxiety and stress is on the rise. Therefore, it is important for us to be empathetic towards one another and check up on each other to assure the team that we all are in this together and things will get better soon.

3. Team Building Activities:

Working remotely is a new practice. For the majority of the workers as well as the companies, they are facing this situation for the first time. Therefore, employees are finding it a bit hard to adjust to this change and remote team management is an issue.

Setting up some time and looking for an interesting remote team building activity and conduct it with your employees. There are several team building activities that can be arranged online. Each activity has its own goal. Some skills that can be taught to the employees through these activities to boost the morale of the workers and motivate them to work include:

  • Time management
  • Effective communication
  • Team building
  • Cooperation and co-working
  • Leadership

These activities bring the team together and promote an overall culture of cooperation and working together for the employees. This promotes a sense of togetherness and thus motivates the employees to do well. The employees will also feel empowered and this practice will also portray a positive company culture.

4. Set Rewards:

If you are looking for the answer to how to motivate employees during covid-19, one amazing way can be through rewards. It is human nature that we feel motivated to work and push through when we are offered any type of incentive or award.

Try doing the same for your employees. Set goals and targets, and set the teams to work. When the targets are achieved and the goals are met, make sure to praise the employees and reward them for their efforts. Your small words of encouragement will push the employees and serve as a motivating force to help them overcome any hurdles that are becoming the reason for demotivation. The rewards you offer can be encouragement and applause, monetary incentives or even other company benefits that the employees may be interested in. You will be amazed to see what a few words of encouragement can lead to and how they impact the employee productivity and motivation!

5. Allow Breaks:

The Covid virus pandemic is surely an unprecedented time. Everyone is facing a severe situation they never even imagined they would ever be stuck in. Therefore, bear in mind that things will get tough for your employees.

Everyone is struggling with not only ensuring their physical well-being but also to maintain a stable mental health. There are days where one is feeling extremely low and cannot get the energy to do anything. But at the same time, there are some days where the productivity is skyrocketed and a lot can be accomplished within the same day.

Give your employees some space and understand their situation. Set a flexible work routine and expect breaks. Companies cannot expect remote teams to work as productively as in-house teams, especially given the current situation and level of stress and uncertainty.

Name: 6 Things You Can Do to Avoid Zoom Fatigue
Introduction Paragraph: Difficulty concentrating; irritability, muscle tension, tiredness. Why Zoom meetings are so exhausting and 6 things you can do to reduce the fatigue.
Post Body:
4:53 PM. You just hit the “Leave Meeting” button on your sixth and final video conference of the day. You think about sending an email to confirm the meeting’s action items, but you’re too tired and scattered to remember what they were. Your eyes are dry and sore. Your shoulders are stiff. You’re grouchy and can’t understand why you’re so drained when all you’ve been doing is sitting in front of your laptop all day. This is Zoom Fatigue.

One thing the COVID-19 lockdown taught us is that we must take care of our mental health as well as our physical health. Somewhat ironically, another thing the pandemic taught us is that, when it’s not safe to go into the office, we can work from home and be in virtual meetings all day, every day. Whether we realize it or not, communicating via videoconference demands more brain power than meeting in person. And doing it for hours on end can start to take a toll on a person’s mental health.

What is Zoom Fatigue?

Before we get into “symptoms,” let’s zoom out (see what we did there?) for a moment and talk about videoconferencing itself. Virtual meetings are not inherently a bad thing. On the contrary, videoconferencing platforms are wonderful tools for team collaboration and communication. They have been especially vital to staying productive and collaborating while working remotely, as the pandemic has forced many of us to do. However, the rise of Zoom fatigue has shown that we should develop healthy habits in our use of videoconferencing.

The signs of Zoom fatigue are similar to those of classic occupational burnout:

  • Difficulty concentrating
  • Lack of motivation
  • Irritability toward co-workers (which may spill into personal relationships)
  • Feeling tired or exhausted
  • Even physical symptoms such as muscle tension, headache, etc

Also, it should be noted that even though the term has been coined as “Zoom fatigue,” it is used as a blanket term not exclusive to Zoom. It can refer to exhaustion related to prolonged use of any videoconferencing platform, including Microsoft Teams, RingCentral, Slack, GoToMeeting, Cisco Webex, etc.

Why are online meetings more draining than in-person meetings?

Researchers at Stanford University published an article that posits “nonverbal overload” as a possible explanation for Zoom fatigue. To explain what is meant by nonverbal overload, think about the things that are associated with being on a videoconference:

  • The prolonged, intense eye contact with people we are not usually intimate with
  • The extreme close-up interaction with enlarged, disembodied faces
  • The mirror effect of constantly seeing your own face in virtual meetings
  • The effort of keeping your body relatively immobile to stay on camera
  • The reduced ability to read nonverbal cues like body language, gestures and tone

All the above behaviors are quite unnatural to normal human interaction. As a consequence, our brains have to work harder to process them. Although we may not consciously realize it, virtual meetings are quite the mental workout, and we’re doing them for extended periods throughout the workday.

Another factor that can contribute to Zoom fatigue is the mixing of home and work environments. It can be embarrassing or even nerve wracking to be in a meeting while having to worry about your kids crying, your cat jumping on the keyboard, or your neighbor cranking the lawn mower. And while many videoconferencing platforms offer virtual backgrounds, they can sometimes glitch or fail, exposing your messy living room.

What can I do to avoid Zoom fatigue?

Again, Zoom fatigue is not a direct effect of videoconferencing itself, but rather a reaction to repeated and prolonged meetings on camera. There are several steps we can take to reduce or avoid the exhaustion.

  1. Go off camera: If your employer doesn’t require you to be on camera, don’t feel obligated to turn it on. Better yet, switch to phone or email.
  2. Take camera breaks: If video is required, perhaps you can limit camera time and turn it on only when speaking or interacting.
  3. Take screen breaks too: When your camera is off, face away from the screen to give yourself a break from looking at everyone else too.
  4. Reduce window size: Shrink the other attendant’s faces down to minimize the illusion of them being so close to your face.
  5. Hide self-view: If the platform allows, turn off the self-view feature. If not, slap a post-it note over your face.
  6. Take REAL breaks: Between meetings, step away from the screen and give yourself a real break. Take a walk. Drink some water. Give your dog a belly rub.

Even though things are slowly returning to normal and businesses are starting to re-open their offices, videoconferencing is here to stay. Making these small adjustments can have a big impact in your comfort and well-being at work, which will in turn effect your overall mental wellness.

Name: 6 Ways to Protect Against Smishing & Text Spam
Introduction Paragraph: Text spam is not only annoying, but also harmful. Smishing is a text spam technique that can hack your device and data. Here’s how to prevent it.
Post Body:
Spam text messages are not only annoying... they can also be dangerous! Cybercriminals use text messages to trick you into entering your personal information. This is known as smishing (“SMS phishing”).

There are several approaches used to try to compel a user to click on a link or submit private information. Some trick users into thinking they are being linked to tracking information for a delivery. Some send messages telling a user that their electric bill is unpaid and they must submit payment information to avoid disconnection. Some even try to embarrass a user into clicking a link with a message like, “OMG is this you in this video??” During the COVID-19 pandemic, cybercriminals even preyed on people by pretending to offer help.

Here are 6 tips on how to protect yourself against smishing and text spam:

  1. Verify the sender & take a moment to think about why they are asking for your information.
  2. Don’t click on any links provided in the text message and do not reply.
  3. Block the number. Follow these links for instructions on Android or on iPhone.
  4. Forward the message to 7226 to report it to your carrier.
  5. Use security software to protect against malware.
  6. Update your phone’s operating system to ensure it has the latest security patches.

Smishing is just one way your mobile device can be attacked. If you didn’t see our blog post last week regarding mobile security, catch up here: 12 Tips for Avoiding Mobile Security Threats.
