Name: Benefits of on-premise Phone Systems 2020
Introduction Paragraph: On-premise phone systems offer many advantages over cloud-based solutions. Find out how they can improve your business communication and security.
Post Body:
Business owners can often be found reminiscing about the best phone system for small business. The two most popular choices are on-premise phone systems and cloud based phone services. There are plenty of best on premise VOIP system options to choose from. Although the on-premise phone systems come with a larger upfront cost to set up the service, they are still recommended because of the reduced risk of security issues and the large degrees of customizability. Before making the decision between on premise phone system vs cloud, it is important that you compare the benefits, features, pros and cons of both so you can make the right choice depending on the needs of your business. Many people assume that on-premise phone systems are outdated and no longer have any sort of VOIP features and benefits, in reality, they are still quite relevant and are used by many small scale businesses around the globe.

There seems to be an ongoing debate about whether on-premise or cloud based phone systems are the most appropriate choice for businesses and enterprises. After all communication is the backbone for any enterprise and it is important to ensure uninterrupted connectivity at all times. If you are someone who is going back and forth about cloud vs on premise phone systems this article will prove to be extremely helpful for you. We will go over the details of both cloud as well as on-premise phone systems and compare them to analyze the benefits of on-premise phone systems 2020.

What is an on-premise phone system?

On-premise phone systems are also known as PBX. This term simply refers to a physical phone system that is owned by the business itself. The phone is available on the business’s premise and the business is responsible for the maintenance, upgrade and overall upkeep of the phone system.

An on-premise phone system has a lot to offer for the businesses. From digital setup to voice over internet protocol services, a diverse range of features can be associated with a best on-premise phone system. When a business opts for an on-premise phone system they get the full control over the features and the extent of the services and settings of the phone. It means there is more responsibility but at the same time, the degree of customizability is worth the effort.

What is a cloud phone system?

On the other hand, the cloud phone system relies on internet connection rather than the traditional analog phone communication channels. The internet connectivity ensures that you can easily communicate with people from all around the globe. There is no restriction based on the location of the clients or the business. Since internet connectivity is easily available, therefore, businesses can communicate with their clients with ease.

Another perk associated with the use of a cloud phone system for business is the reduced cost. When compared with an on-premise phone system, the cloud phone systems seem to be a far more affordable choice. In case of a cloud phone system, the service provider is responsible for the upgrades, fine tuning and other settings of the phone system. There is not much room to customize the service according to the needs of your business but since everything is the responsibility of the service provider, the business does not have to put in any effort in this regard.

On-premise phone systems vs cloud phone systems:

Now that we have briefly discussed both on-premise as well as cloud phone systems, we can now move on and address the elephant in the room which is hosted vs on premise phone system which one is a better choice. Here is a detailed comparison of both a premise based phone system and a cloud based phone system.

Flexibility:

When you opt for an on-premise phone system, the payments have to be made upfront. Not everyone who will be reaching out to you via the phone service is a valuable customer. There are plenty of calls that you might be receiving that may prove to be redundant and unnecessary at the end of the day. But since you will be paying upfront, you will have to bear the cost of these calls as well. Moreover, you will determine a specific number of callers to pay for. Changing this number of callers as the needs of your business fluctuate might be hassle in case of using an on-premise phone service.

In case of a cloud phone system, the businesses only have to pay for the features that are actually being used. Therefore, growing the extent of the services or contracting them is easier. Therefore, making use of an on cloud phone system is better for businesses whose needs keep fluctuating.

Scope Of Offices:

If your business is divided into multiple office locations, you will have to make sure that the offices are connected and can communicate with ease. On-premise phone systems can allow connections over remote locations but the connection requires special hardware arrangements and can be expensive.

But if you opt for the cloud based phone systems, connecting remote offices will be extremely simple and easy. You can simply make use of one cloud phone service provider and have them connect all your offices using the same line.

In a nutshell, if you have a small scale business that comprises a single office, the on-premise phone system would be sufficient enough to meet your needs. But in case your office setup is divided amongst different locations, connecting them via a cloud phone system would be the better option.

Extent Of Controls:

Another significant factor that you need to take into consideration when determining whether you should go for an on-premise or cloud based phone system is the extent of controls you require. If you want to take control of every setting and features, opting for the on-premise phone systems would be ideal for you. On-premise phone systems are completely managed by the business itself. Therefore, you have the freedom to customize the extent of the services as you like.

However, if you are someone who does not mind a more generalized phone service, you can choose a cloud based phone system. The controls and settings will be in the hands of the service provider but you can always reach out to them in case you want to make any change.

Tech and IT Department:

As already discussed earlier, an on-premise phone system is demanding and you have to take care of all the technical details and responsibilities yourself. If you have an established tech and IT department that can manage the best on-premise VOIP phone system, you should undoubtedly prefer the on-premise phone setup.

However, if you are in the initial phase of setting up your business and therefore do not have a dedicated team or department that can manage the phone, it is better for you to invest in a cloud based phone system. In case of the cloud based phone system, the service provider will be responsible for the upkeep and the maintenance of the phone and alteration of the services to meet the needs of your business, therefore, this option is the best for the businesses which do not have a team to look after the phone.

Cost:

The cost is a factor that can be divided into two main categories. The first type of cost is the money that will be spent on setting up the service. It is known as the initial setup cost. The initial setup cost in case of a cloud based phone system is lower. This is because you don't need to invest in a lot of hardware to avail this facility. The charges are based on the number of phone lines that will be used on a monthly basis and you can always choose to downgrade or upgrade your package based on the needs of your business. The payments are also scheduled on a monthly basis and the fee includes all the relevant charges like the maintenance and the upkeep expense etc.

On the other hand, the initial setup cost can be higher when setting up an on-premise phone system. This is because the company has to spend money on acquiring the relevant hardware and ensuring that everything is maintained. Some money will also go towards the IT team or department which will be responsible for the smooth running of the on-premise phone system.

Therefore, it is obvious that the on-premise phone system is an expensive choice. It comes with a lot more expenses as well as responsibilities.

Mobility:

As indicated by the name itself, the on-premise phone system is available only within the office premise. Therefore, the employees and the administration can have access to the phone while they are in the same building where the phone system has been deployed. Many on-premise phone systems can be modified to allow remote access but it does come at an additional cost which not many businesses are interested in bearing.

However, the hosted or cloud based phone system is remote. This means that the employees can always stay connected with one another and with the customers or clients as well. The employees do not have to be restricted to the office in order to make use of the phone service.

In terms of productivity, ease of access and mobility, the cloud based phone systems are therefore better and highly recommended.

Security:

When you choose a cloud based phone system, the security of the data and the features etc. is in the hands of the service provider. Therefore, it is critical for the business to be vigilant and choose the right service provider in this regard. Make sure that the service provider makes use of two step authentication and encryption to ensure that the data remains secure and free from any risk.

However, in case of a cloud based phone system, the business’s IT department or the team handling the services is responsible for the security. This gives the businesses freedom to choose security protocols and ensure a foolproof mechanism to secure the phone’s data.

If you choose the right service provider, security would not be a concern. But when you are trusting a third party for this service, the security risks are higher as compared to the on-premise phone systems where the business itself can take care of the security.

Data Recovery In Case of Disaster:

Cloud based phone systems have the data stored on a cloud storage location. Therefore, the recovery of data in case of a natural or physical disaster is easy. But this is not the case when an on-premise phone system is taken into consideration. All the data is stored within the office location. If anything was to go wrong or the business meets any disaster, the data recovery will not be an easy task. And in some cases, it might even be impossible for one to be able to recover the data.  If you want to opt for an on-premise phone system and ensure that your data also remains safe, make sure that you backup the data regularly to have a disaster recovery plan at hand. This will prepare you to deal with any type of unplanned disaster.

Conclusion:

Both on-premise and cloud based phone systems have their own pros and cons. Which option is the best for you depends on the scope and needs of your business. The choice of an on-premise phone system is well suited for you if you want to have full control over the service and can take the responsibility of managing the phone. The major benefits of an on-premise phone system is the degree of customizability as well as the fact that security is not a concern. Moreover, if you have a small scale business where the need of remote based phone systems is not a concern, the on-premise phone system will be an optimal choice for you. It may come with a higher upfront cost but in the long term, it will be a great investment that will benefit the business greatly.

Name: Best Data Backup Solutions: Cloud Backup 2024
Introduction Paragraph: Discover the top data backup solutions for 2024, including cloud backup services and enterprise software. Protect your digital assets effectively with expert insights.
Post Body:
In the digital age, where data reigns supreme, safeguarding your valuable information has never been more critical. With the ever-present threat of data loss looming over individuals and businesses alike, having the best data backup solution is not just a luxury; it's a necessity.

In this comprehensive guide, we'll delve into the world of data backup, exploring what it is, why it's essential, and the best solutions available in 2024.

What is data backup?

Before we dive into the intricacies of the best data backup solutions, let's first understand what data backup entails. In simple terms, data backup refers to the process of creating a duplicate copy of your digital information to ensure its availability and integrity in the event of data loss or corruption.

This backup copy serves as a safety net, allowing you to restore your data to its original state following any unforeseen mishaps.

How do online backup services work?

Online backup services allow users to upload their data to remote servers maintained by the service provider. This process typically involves installing backup software on the user's device, selecting the files or folders to be backed up, and initiating the backup process.

Once the data is uploaded to the remote servers, users can access and restore their data from any internet-connected device, ensuring its availability in the event of data loss or device failure.

Types of data backup

Data backup comes in various forms, each offering unique advantages and catering to different needs. Some of the most common types of data backup include:

Full backup

A full backup involves copying all data files and folders from a source to a destination. While comprehensive, full backups can be time-consuming and resource-intensive, particularly for large datasets.

Incremental backup

Incremental backups capture only the changes made since the last backup, reducing the time and storage space required compared to full backups. However, restoring data from incremental backups may involve multiple steps, as it relies on the latest full backup and subsequent incremental backups.

Differential backup

Similar to incremental backups, a differential backup captures changes made since the last full backup. However, unlike incremental backups, which only store changes since the last backup, differential backups accumulate changes since the last full backup, making restoration faster and simpler.

Cloud backup

Cloud backup, also known as online backup, leverages remote servers and internet connectivity to store backup copies of data. This approach offers scalability, accessibility, and off-site storage, making it an increasingly popular choice for individuals and businesses alike.

The role of data backup and recovery services

In today's data-driven world, the role of data backup and recovery services cannot be overstated. These services provide peace of mind by offering automated backups, robust security measures, and efficient data restoration capabilities.

Whether you're an individual looking to protect cherished memories or an enterprise safeguarding critical business operations, data backup and recovery services play a vital role in mitigating the risk of data loss and ensuring continuity.

Crafting a data backup plan

Regardless of the backup solution you choose, developing a comprehensive data backup plan is essential to safeguarding your digital assets effectively. Here are some key steps to consider when crafting a data backup plan:

  • Assessment of backup needs: Evaluate your data storage requirements, including the amount of data to be backed up, retention policies, and compliance considerations.
  • Selection of backup solution: Choose a backup solution that aligns with your budget, scalability requirements, and data protection goals, whether it's cloud-based, on-premises, or a hybrid approach.
  • Implementation of backup strategy: Deploy your chosen backup solution and configure backup schedules, retention policies, and security measures to ensure optimal protection of your data.
  • Testing and validation: Regularly test your backup systems and procedures to verify their effectiveness and identify any potential weaknesses or vulnerabilities.
  • Continual monitoring and optimization: Monitor the performance of your backup solution, update backup policies as needed, and incorporate emerging technologies and best practices to enhance your data protection strategy continually.

By following these steps and investing in the best data backup solution, you can mitigate the risk of data loss, protect your valuable information, and ensure business continuity in the face of unforeseen challenges.

Best data backup solutions of 2024

With an array of data backup solutions available in the market, choosing the right one can be a daunting task. To help you navigate this landscape, we've curated a list of the best data backup solutions for 2024:

1. Cloud backup services

  • Dropbox Backup: As one of the pioneers in cloud storage, Dropbox offers the best data backup solution for individuals and businesses alike. With seamless file synchronization, versioning, and strong encryption, Dropbox Backup ensures your data remains safe and accessible from anywhere.
  • Google Drive: Google Drive stands out for its integration with other Google Workspace applications, making it an excellent choice for businesses already utilizing Google's ecosystem. With features like real-time collaboration and advanced search capabilities, Google Drive provides a versatile backup solution for diverse needs.
  • B2 Cloud Storage: Backblaze's B2 Cloud Storage is a cost-effective option for businesses requiring scalable cloud storage with reliable backup capabilities. With competitive pricing and straightforward pricing models, B2 Cloud Storage is an attractive choice for businesses of all sizes.

2. Enterprise backup software

  • Barracuda Backup: Barracuda offers comprehensive backup solutions tailored to the needs of enterprises, combining backup and recovery capabilities with advanced security features. With support for physical, virtual, and cloud environments, Barracuda Backup ensures data protection across diverse IT infrastructures.
  • Acronis Cyber Backup: Acronis Cyber Backup provides a comprehensive backup and recovery solution with advanced features such as image-based backup, ransomware protection, and blockchain-based authentication. Designed for businesses of all sizes, Acronis Cyber Backup offers scalability and flexibility to adapt to evolving data protection needs.

3. Online backup services

  • Carbonite: Carbonite is a user-friendly online backup service ideal for individuals and small businesses seeking hassle-free data protection. With automatic backups, unlimited storage, and easy file recovery, Carbonite offers peace of mind without the complexity.
  • Backblaze: Backblaze stands out for its straightforward pricing and unlimited storage options, making it attractive for users with large backup needs. With continuous backup, file versioning, and robust security measures, Backblaze provides reliable data protection for individuals and businesses.

4. Hybrid backup solutions

  • Veeam Backup & Replication: Veeam offers a comprehensive hybrid backup solution designed to meet the needs of modern enterprises. With support for virtual, physical, and cloud environments, Veeam Backup & Replication provides flexibility and scalability to ensure data protection across diverse IT infrastructures.
  • Commvault: Commvault's hybrid backup solution combines on-premises and cloud-based backup capabilities to provide comprehensive data protection. With features such as deduplication, encryption, and disaster recovery, Commvault offers a robust backup solution for businesses seeking maximum resilience.

5. SaaS backup solutions

  • Druva: Druva specializes in SaaS backup solutions tailored to the needs of cloud-centric businesses. With support for leading SaaS applications like Microsoft 365 and Salesforce, Druva ensures data protection and compliance for cloud-based workflows.
  • Spanning Backup: Spanning Backup offers automated backup and recovery solutions for popular SaaS applications, including G Suite and Microsoft 365. With granular restore capabilities and advanced security features, Spanning Backup provides peace of mind for businesses relying on cloud-based productivity tools.

Choose the best data backup solution

The best backup solution for 2024 offers a combination of reliability, scalability, and security to meet the diverse needs of individuals and businesses. Whether you opt for cloud backup services, enterprise backup software, online backup services, hybrid solutions, or SaaS backup solutions, prioritizing data protection is essential in safeguarding your digital assets and ensuring business continuity in an increasingly data-driven world.

Factors to consider in choosing the best data backup solution

  • Reliability: The best data backup solution should offer reliable and consistent backup performance, ensuring that your data is protected without fail. Look for solutions with proven track records and reliability guarantees.
  • Security: Data security is paramount in today's digital landscape. Choose a backup solution that employs robust encryption standards and security protocols to safeguard your sensitive information from unauthorized access and cyber threats.
  • Scalability: As your data volume grows over time, your backup solution should be able to scale accordingly. Opt for a solution that offers scalability to accommodate your evolving storage needs without compromising performance or reliability.
  • Ease of use: A user-friendly interface and intuitive setup process can make all the difference in the adoption and usability of a backup solution. Look for solutions that are easy to deploy, manage, and monitor, minimizing the learning curve for users.
  • Speed: Backup and restore speeds are critical, especially for businesses with stringent downtime requirements. Choose a solution that offers fast backup and recovery times to minimize disruption to your operations in the event of data loss.
  • Cost-effectiveness: While cost should not be the sole determining factor, it's essential to choose a backup solution that offers value for money. Consider factors such as pricing structure, total cost of ownership, and potential savings in the event of data loss.
  • Compliance and regulations: Depending on your industry and geographic location, you may be subject to specific regulatory requirements regarding data protection and privacy. Choose the best data backup solution that complies with relevant regulations and offers features such as data encryption and retention policies to ensure compliance.

Choosing the best online backup services: Safeguarding your digital assets

In conclusion, data backup is a fundamental aspect of modern computing, enabling individuals and businesses to protect their digital assets from loss, corruption, and unauthorized access.

By understanding the importance of data backup, exploring which is the best data backup solution, and implementing a robust backup strategy, you can safeguard your data effectively and maintain peace of mind in an increasingly data-centric world.

Frequently asked questions

What is a cloud backup solution?

A cloud backup solution refers to the process of backing up data to a remote, off-site server over the Internet. This approach offers scalability, accessibility, and off-site storage, making it an increasingly popular choice for individuals and businesses alike.

With a cloud backup solution, you can securely store your data in the cloud, ensuring its availability and integrity in the event of data loss or corruption.

How does a cloud backup solution differ from a local backup?

While local backup involves storing data on physical storage devices such as external hard drives or network-attached storage (NAS) devices, a cloud backup solution utilizes remote servers and internet connectivity for data storage.

Unlike local backup, which may be limited by storage capacity and physical location, cloud backup solutions offer scalability, accessibility, and off-site storage, making them an ideal choice for users seeking flexibility and redundancy.

What are the benefits of using cloud backup services?

Cloud backup services offer several benefits, including:

  • Scalability: Cloud backup services allow you to scale your storage capacity up or down as needed, ensuring that you can accommodate the growing volume of data over time.
  • Accessibility: With cloud backup services, you can access your data from anywhere with an internet connection, enabling remote access and collaboration.
  • Redundancy: Cloud backup services replicate your data across multiple servers and data centers, providing redundancy and minimizing the risk of data loss due to hardware failures or disasters.
  • Automatic backup: Many cloud backup services offer automatic backup capabilities, ensuring that your data is backed up regularly without manual intervention.

How can I choose the best backup services for my needs?

When choosing the best cloud backup services, consider factors such as:

  • Reliability: Look for cloud backup services with a proven track record of reliability and uptime.
  • Security: Ensure that the cloud backup service employs robust encryption and security measures to protect your data from unauthorized access.
  • Cost: Evaluate the pricing structure and total cost of ownership of the cloud backup service, taking into account factors such as storage capacity, data transfer fees, and any additional features or add-ons.
  • Features: Assess the features and capabilities offered by the cloud backup service, such as automatic backup, file versioning, and data recovery options.

Can I use cloud backup services for enterprise backup and recovery?

Yes, many cloud backup services cater to the needs of enterprises and offer robust backup and recovery solutions. These services provide features such as deduplication, encryption, and disaster recovery capabilities to ensure the integrity and availability of critical business data.

By leveraging cloud backup services for enterprise backup and recovery, organizations can enhance data protection, scalability, and accessibility while minimizing infrastructure costs and complexity.

What role do cloud storage services play in data backup?

Cloud storage services play a crucial role in data backup by providing secure, scalable, and off-site storage for backup copies of data. These services offer features such as versioning, encryption, and access controls, enabling users to store and retrieve their data reliably and efficiently.

By leveraging cloud storage services for data backup, users can benefit from redundancy, accessibility, and cost-effectiveness, ensuring the safety and availability of their valuable information.

How can I protect my data with backup storage solutions?

To protect your data with backup storage solutions, consider the following tips:

  • Regular backups: Implement a regular backup schedule to ensure that your data is backed up consistently and reliably.
  • Encryption: Use encryption to secure your backup data and protect it from unauthorized access or data breaches.
  • Off-site storage: Store backup copies of your data off-site, either in the cloud or in a secure location away from your primary data center, to mitigate the risk of data loss due to disasters or physical damage.
  • Testing and validation: Regularly test and validate your backup storage solutions to ensure that they are functioning correctly and that your data can be restored quickly and effectively in the event of data loss.

How do backup providers ensure the security and integrity of my data?

Backup providers employ various security measures and best practices to ensure the security and integrity of your data, including:

  • Encryption: Backup providers use encryption to protect your data during transit and at rest, ensuring that it remains secure and inaccessible to unauthorized parties.
  • Access controls: Backup providers implement access controls and authentication mechanisms to restrict access to your data and prevent unauthorized users from tampering with or accessing sensitive information.
  • Compliance: Backup providers adhere to industry standards and regulatory requirements regarding data protection and privacy, ensuring compliance with relevant laws and regulations.
  • Monitoring and auditing: Backup providers monitor and audit their systems and infrastructure regularly to detect and mitigate security threats, ensuring the ongoing security and integrity of your data.

Name: Best File Sharing Software Platforms For Business In 2020
Introduction Paragraph: Strong files and data on a physical storage space is great, but it creates issues when you try to share the files or collaborate on them in real time
Post Body:
Strong files and data on a physical storage space is great, but it creates issues when you try to share the files or collaborate on them in real time. This is why businesses and enterprises are often looking for file sharing programs that can help them in this regard. There are plenty of free file sharing sites that can prove helpful for file sharing and storage. These sites provide businesses with cloud storage that they can utilize to hold their files and also provides them with a chance to share their files for online collaboration. There are plenty of free online file sharing and storage platforms for you to choose from however, make sure that you select one that is truly reliable in terms of safety and security. We have shared the top five best free file sharing software that we have tried, tested and found extremely helpful in this regard.

Businesses and companies are heavily dependent on corporate file sharing. Therefore they are always looking for the best file share software for business. The file sharing software should ensure a quick and hassle free file transfer. At the same time, it should be safe as well so one does not have to be concerned about the safety of the files being shared.

In order to ensure effective online file sharing, it is important that you choose the right document sharing applications. The biggest perk of the online file sharing platforms is the fact that they make collaboration quite simple and hassle free. Moreover, since the documents and other data is stored on a cloud based server, you can easily access it from anywhere and it is not restricted to a physical storage location.

If you are looking for a reliable and best file sharing platform, keep reading as we have compiled a list of the top five best file sharing software platforms for businesses in 2020.

1. Files.com:

If you are looking for a cloud based server for small business, files.com would be a great choice for you. The platform not only allows users to store their files online, but the files can also be protected and shared via a link. Users also have the option of creating customized links for each person you share the file with. The option of access control is also integrated with the file sharing so that you can control whether the other person can view, edit or download the file.

Clients can choose to use the files.com cloud storage or integrate it with other cloud storage platforms like Google Drive, OneDrive and Dropbox etc. To make the file sharing secure and safe, this online document sharing portal also encrypts the files and protects the transfer using SSH or TLS protocols. All the accounts created on files.com are secured by two factor authentication and the risk of anyone bypassing their strong security protocols is close to zero.

Files.com is a paid web based file sharing platform. Users can choose between three different subscription packages. The packages differ on the basis of the storage and data limit that the users are offered per month. However, if you want to check out the services of the platform before signing up for the subscription, you can sign up for a free trial and check the service for one month.

2. FileWhopper:

If you do not want to sign up for a long term file sharing service and are looking for a more affordable option in this regard, you should give FileWhopper a try. It is a service that charges the customers on the basis of their usage. The usage and the interface of this best file transfer software for pc is also quite simple which makes it easy to use.

All you need to do is to upload the file that you want to share on the FileWhopper platform. A link will be automatically generated. You can simply share this link with the person with whom you want to share the file. The payment is billed based on the size of the file and the number of files that you upload and share. Therefore, you will be able to cut down on the cost of cloud storage business and pay for only the services that you actually use.

The files uploaded are not stored on the FileWhopper server permanently. Rather, the system is flushed after two weeks and the files uploaded are deleted.

It is important to bear in mind that FileWhopper is a file transfer platform and it does not support live collaboration or file storage. There is no limit on the size of the file that can be uploaded by the user since the billing is based on the use rather than a fixed subscription plan so it does allow a lot of customizability and freedom to the users. You will see the price quote as soon as you start uploading the file so you will be informed about the cost beforehand.

3. Dropbox Business:

If you are looking for a best business file sharing platform, Dropbox Business would be a great option. It is a one stop solution for all your business file storage and file sharing needs. The best feature of this best file sharing software for pc is the easy access. You can access your dropbox files using your laptop, pc and even your mobile phone. Whenever a file is uploaded, edited or removed from Dropbox, it is updated across all devices immediately so you can be sure that you are accessing the latest version of the files.

For the business customers, they have also introduced Dropbox business that allows business files to be stored at one place and everyone in the company can access the files. There is also an option of permission controls that allows business account holders to manage which user can have what extent of access to the files.

Dropbox Business offers a total of six subscription plans. Three are aimed for personal use whereas there are three which are specially designed for enterprise use. These do have a limited storage space. However, if you want to access unlimited storage, you can sign up for the advanced version that does not have any limit on the storage that is available. There is a thirty day trial period that users can sign up for and test the quality of this file transfer service before they go ahead and pay for the dropbox business account pricing.

4. G Suite Google Drive:

G Suit Google Drive is one of the most popular and commonly used best large file transfer channels. It does offer free storage space if you sign up for a personal account. The access of the files stored on the Google Drive is also efficient as you can access the Google Drive files and folders from any device that has the account signed in and has an active internet connection.

The most prominent feature of the Google Drive file transfer platform that makes it so popular is the fact that it supports live collaboration. You can upload documents or even create presentations, spreadsheets and text documents directly on the drive. You can choose to share it with people and they can either only view, edit or comment on the files based on their permissions. Even a group of people can come together and collaborate on the files. There is no limit to the number of people with whom the files can be shared at a time.

Users are given a total of 15 GB free storage space on the drive. But if you want more storage, you can also sign up for the paid version and increase the storage space. The charges are based on the storage space and not the number or size of files which surely makes it one of the most recommended best cloud storage for photos.

5. OneDrive For Business:

The OneDrive free file host is owned by Microsoft. It allows you to share files with other people in a safe, secure and efficient manner. You can choose to store the files on any device, but as long as they are uploaded to this best free file sharing software you will be able to access it from any device. Any change that is made to the files is automatically synced and therefore the files are updated and you will be able to access the updated files with ease.

You can use Microsoft OneDrive to store files, folders, pictures, videos and any other data that you would like to store or share. In order to share the folder with anyone, all you need to do is to share the link of the folder with the person with whom you want to share the file. If you want to restrict the access to any file, you can use the expiration feature and set a time limit after which the access to the file will expire and the recipient will not be able to access the file.

OneDrive can also be paired with phone scanning apps so that you can scan documents and directly upload them on the storage rather than having to store them on the device first and then upload them on the drive afterwards. OneDrive does offer a couple of price packages depending on the extent of services provided and you can pick one that best suits the needs of your business.

Name: Boosting Business Success with Prompt Engineering: A Game-Changer for SMBs
Introduction Paragraph: Discover the power of prompt engineering for businesses. Learn how this technique empowers SMBs to leverage AI and gain a competitive edge.
Post Body:
In the fast-paced world of technology, businesses of all sizes strive to stay ahead of the competition. Small to medium-sized businesses (SMBs) face unique challenges when it comes to leveraging cutting-edge AI models. However, with the advent of prompt engineering, SMBs now have a powerful tool in their hands to level the playing field. In this blog post, we will explore what prompt engineering is, and how it can benefit SMBs. We will also provide a set of practical tips to help SMBs effectively implement prompt engineering in their businesses.

Understanding Prompt Engineering: Prompt engineering is the art of formulating precise and effective instructions, or prompts, to interact with AI models. It involves crafting input text that helps guide the AI model's output in a desired direction. By mastering the skill of prompt engineering, businesses can harness the power of AI to address a wide range of challenges, from automating mundane tasks to improving decision-making processes.

4 Benefits of Prompt Engineering for SMBs:

  1. Enhanced Efficiency: SMBs often have limited resources and need to maximize their productivity. With prompt engineering, businesses can leverage AI models to automate repetitive tasks, streamline workflows, and free up valuable time for employees to focus on high-value activities.
  2. Improved Customer Experience: Providing exceptional customer service is crucial for SMBs to build a loyal customer base. Prompt engineering allows businesses to use AI models to generate personalized responses, recommend products or services, and offer tailored solutions, thereby enhancing the overall customer experience.
  3. Data-Driven Decision Making: SMBs often struggle to gather actionable insights from vast amounts of data. Prompt engineering can help businesses extract meaningful information from data by formulating prompts that guide AI models to analyze, summarize, and visualize data, enabling better decision-making and driving business growth.
  4. Competitive Advantage: SMBs can gain a competitive edge by leveraging AI capabilities through prompt engineering. Whether it's improving marketing campaigns, optimizing supply chains, or predicting customer behavior, prompt engineering enables SMBs to access advanced AI models and compete with larger enterprises.

5 Practical Tips for Effective Prompt Engineering:

  1. Clearly Define Your Goal: Before crafting a prompt, clearly define the objective you want to achieve. Be specific about the desired outcome and consider the limitations and scope of the AI model you are using.
  2. Experiment and Iterate: Prompt engineering is an iterative process. Experiment with different prompts and iterate based on the results you obtain. Continuously refine and fine-tune your prompts to achieve the desired outputs.
  3. Use Contextual Information: Utilize relevant contextual information in your prompts to guide the AI model's understanding. Providing specific examples, constraints, or domain-specific knowledge can significantly improve the accuracy and relevance of the generated outputs.
  4. Consider Bias and Fairness: AI models can inadvertently reflect biases present in the data they were trained on. Ensure that your prompts are designed to mitigate bias and promote fairness in the generated outputs. Regularly monitor and review the results to identify and address any potential biases.
  5. Collaborate with Experts: Engage domain experts within your organization or seek external assistance to craft prompts that align with your business objectives. Their expertise can help you create more effective prompts and generate optimal results.

Prompt engineering has opened up new avenues for SMBs to leverage AI technologies and accelerate their growth. By mastering the art of prompt engineering, SMBs can automate tasks, enhance customer experiences, make data-driven decisions, and gain a competitive advantage. Implementing the practical tips mentioned in this blog post will help SMBs unlock the full potential of AI and drive their businesses towards success in today's rapidly evolving digital landscape. Embrace prompt engineering and unleash the power of AI to propel your SMB to new heights.

Name: Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet for Business
Introduction Paragraph: Businesses today increasingly rely on, and in a vast majority of cases, can’t even survive without, a fast and reliable Internet connection.
Post Body:
Most businesses survive on the same set of basic needs. The things that, if they were to suddenly become unavailable, would force a business to shut down for the day and send everyone home. These things include: a building or facility, electricity and internet. This post covers the two main types of internet service, the differences between the two, and the pros and cons of each.

Businesses rely on the Internet to run their web-based line-of-business applications, to virtually meet with their teams and clients, to share documents and collaborate in the cloud... the list goes on and on. With this much on the line, the type of internet service you choose can have a profound impact on your business’ performance and productivity.

What’s the difference between broadband and fiber?

There are two main types of internet service: Broadband and Fiber. Broadband is the standard type of high-speed, always-available internet access and comes in several different forms, most commonly DSL and cable. Fiber internet uses fiber-optic cables instead of copper wire to deliver service.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Technology

As previously mentioned, there are several different types of broadband, each with their own technology. DSL transmits data over traditional copper telephone lines. Cable broadband transmits data through copper coaxial wire used for cable television. Both DSL and cable broadband are built on top of existing phone and cable TV networks. Since phone and cable networks are already widespread, these types of broadband internet are readily available to most of the world.

Fiber internet transmits data via pulses of light through flexible strands of glass inside an insulated casing. Since the data travels literally at the speed of light, this provides a much faster connection. However, since the technology is newer, fiber networks are not as widespread and may take longer to provision.

Overall, fiber is the newer and more robust technology, but it’s not as widely available as broadband.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Bandwidth

Bandwidth is the rate at which data can be transferred within a specific unit of time. This is usually expressed in bits per second.

Broadband Internet typically offers decent bandwidth, but each type has its limitations. DSL connections are distance-sensitive, so the further away you are from the service provider’s switching station, the slower the connection will be. In cable connections, bandwidth is shared with other customers, potentially including residential customers. This means the connection can be significantly slower if more people are using it at the same time. DSL and cable internet is asymmetrical, so upload speeds are much slower than download speeds.

Fiber has greater bandwidth than any of the other broadband options (remember “speed of light”). Also, fiber has much longer transmission distances than in copper cable, with copper cable losing over 90% of signal strength over 100 meters while fiber loses about 3%. Fiber connections are delivered on a dedicated line, which means that the circuit is provisioned specifically for each customer rather than being shared. Unlike DSL and cable internet, fiber is usually offered with symmetrical service, which means download and upload speeds are the same.

Between broadband and fiber, fiber is the clear winner when it comes to bandwidth and speed. Not only is it inherently faster based on its technology, but it is unaffected by factors that can affect bandwidth in other types of broadband.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Reliability

Broadband is considered to be fairly reliable. However, as previously mentioned, DSL and cable connections use electricity over copper wire to send data, making it vulnerable to interference from nearby power lines or high-voltage equipment. Internet service can also be disrupted in extreme weather conditions, such as thunderstorms.

Since fiber cables are made of glass, they are not susceptible to electrical interference and have a much lower chance of going down during a power outage. Also, fiber can withstand more extreme temperature fluctuations and can be submerged in water, making it virtually weather-proof.

Fiber is lighter, stronger, non-flammable, and much more resilient than broadband, making it the more reliable option.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Value

DSL or cable internet is much cheaper than fiber, as it utilizes existing telephone or cable TV technology and infrastructure.

Fiber circuits tend to be more expensive since they are provisioned specifically for each customer. Also, installation fees for fiber can be higher as the ISP would need to run the dedicated line to the customer’s building (keep in mind this means installation can take longer as well). On its face, fiber is more expensive than broadband when comparing monthly Internet service costs. However, because of fiber’s speed and reliability, a business would be able to work faster and get more done. This would result in lower personnel expenses and increased revenue.

When looking at how internet costs fit into the overall budget of a business, the value of fiber makes it worth the spend.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Target Consumer

Because of the lower cost, reasonable speeds and decent reliability, DSL and cable internet are ideal for domestic/personal use. The majority of home users only use internet for casual browsing, social media and entertainment. High internet reliability at home is nice to have, but usually not critical. If the internet goes down at home, the impact might be you have to wait a bit to watch your favorite TV show. The exception to this, of course, is for users who work remotely from home.

The target consumer for fiber internet is the corporate/business user. Businesses use the internet much more intensely than the average home user. Business internet typically must support cloud computing, line of business applications, file sharing, phone and teleconferencing, collaboration, etc. Also, reliability is a much bigger factor for businesses. If internet becomes unavailable for a business, they usually are not able to function, or only at a severely reduced capacity.

Broadband Internet vs. Fiber Internet: Bottom Line

If your business that relies on the Internet for productivity, communication and collaboration, AllSafe IT strongly recommends dedicated fiber optic Internet service. If you have questions or need assistance finding the right Internet service provider for your business, contact us today!

Name: Business Continuity Solutions For Remote Working In 2020
Introduction Paragraph: Work from home during a crisis can be hard but remote business continuity techniques ensure that the company can move forward and tackle the hardships of the crisis with as little loss as possible.
Post Body:
Almost all businesses and companies have a business continuity plan that can help them deal with unprecedented situations. Since the CoronaVirus pandemic, companies are now moving towards remote working. Therefore, business continuity plan work from home is the need of the hour to ensure business operations can be regulated and goals can met so the company can stay afloat.

Work from home during a crisis can be hard but remote business continuity techniques ensure that the company can move forward and tackle the hardships of the crisis with as little loss as possible.

From managing business resources to taking measures to be able to achieve short term and long term goals, everything is taken into consideration when writing a comprehensive remote work business model. In a nutshell, a business continuity model helps businesses continue operation no matter how many hardships come along the way.

Due to the COVID-19 crisis, companies have now been forced to work from home and adapt to remote working techniques. During this transition from offices to home, business continuity was one of the major concerns for all companies and businesses.

Although remote working is something that was mainly considered an experiment in the past and not many people opted to work from home, given the current scenario, it has become a necessity and one can rightfully assume that it is indeed the “new normal”.

Since this entire shift was urgent and not pre-planned, many companies had to come up with an emergency remote work plan. According to an estimate, one in three companies and businesses have a remote work business model, which makes it obvious why companies and their employees are finding this move challenging and difficult.

What Is A Business Continuity Plan?

We have been hearing so many things about remote working checklist and manuals about how to ensure productivity, goal management and communication when working on a remote basis.

But what the companies and businesses really need to be looking at given the current situation is a way to ensure Business Continuity with Remote Access.

As inferred by the name, a business continuity plan is simply putting in place systems and protocols that can help the business move forward in the times of crisis. In a nutshell, it is simply a disaster management manual that helps mitigate losses and ensure that the business can continue striving even in disasters. It is a comprehensive manual that covers business resources, processes, goals and future plans etc.

What is the main goal of a business? Making sure it remains on top of the competition in the market by retaining existing customers and working to increase the customer base by reaching out to potential customers. Through local business continuity techniques this can be made possible even if the business hits rock bottom and is facing a severe condition such as the CoronaVirus pandemic.

Writing A Business Continuity Plan For Remote Work:

Having a business continuity remote working plan in place is crucial to ensure that the business can continue its operations successfully even if the employees are working from their homes.

Since remote working is a practice that is fairly new to most employees and businesses, it is important to develop a business continuity plan for remote workers so the business can be stirred in the right direction. Here is how one can write a business continuity plan if everyone will be working remotely:

1. Scope Of The Plan:

The first thing to determine when planning business continuity is the scope of the plan. What is the time period for which the planning has to be done? How long will the plan be needed for? What is the duration for which the planning needs to be done? These are some things that need to be taken into consideration when writing a business continuity plan work from home.

2. Key Business Areas:

The next step is to identify the key business areas. How many sections or departments can the business be split into? Once you identify the key business areas, the next goal should be setting aims and objectives for each of these departments. What should be the high priority tasks and which areas can wait for some time should be identified so the necessary actions can be taken accordingly.

3. Identifying Dependencies:

A business or a company is a functional unit that comprises many departments coming together.

Since everyone will be working from home and the collaboration in real time will not be possible, notice the inter dependencies of the different units of the company.

Based on these dependencies and the extent of collaboration, set a plan that helps them communicate with one another and collaborate in a remote working setting.

4. Set Goals:

Setting goals is an integral component of business continuity planning steps for remote working. This helps identify the utmost priorities and also lays a foundation for the work plan for the business. When setting goals, keep both short term as well as long term goals in mind.

When working remotely, the goals also need to be flexible that can be changed as needed and adapt to the needs of the business.

5. Test And Analyze:

As a business owner, you can never be sure whether your remote business continuity techniques are truly working or not. The planning phase of the continuity plan is only theoretical in nature.

Therefore, it has to be put into action to be able to assess its efficiency and highlight the loopholes. When you practically implement the business continuity plan, make sure that you analyze the work from home insights on a regular basis.

This will help you identify the areas that need improvement and also shed light on the aspects that are doing well so the further planning and goal setting can be done in an efficient manner.

Significance Of Remote Work For Business Continuity:

As discussed before, remote working practices have never been adopted before. Therefore, when the entire world was forced to practice isolation and move from working at an office to working from their homes, this came as a hurdle.

This difficult transition paired with the overall anxiety and stress associated with the CoronaVirus pandemic has made things a lot harder.

Therefore, both the employees and the employers need support. There is an air of uncertainty surrounding our lives. During this situation, it is hard to hussle and move forward.

To ensure that the employees can work well and the business too can survive this tough time, it is very important to lay a concrete plan that can help the businesses identify the critical areas and important goals that need to be prioritized. A business continuity plan also helps utilize and deploy business resources in an efficient manner.

One of the biggest challenges associated with working on a remote basis is ensuring productivity and engagement. This problem is solved when a continuity plan is compiled. The plan lists down all the aims, goals and objectives clearly.

As a result, all the members of the team are aware of what needs to be done and can plan their tasks in accordance with the intended targets.

Overcoming Challenges Related To Remote Work:

Many businesses and companies are complaining about being unable to find their way around remote working practices. Both the employees as well as the employers are facing difficulties.

The biggest aid that can help bridge the gaps between colleagues is ensuring regular and efficient communication. Make use of virtual collaboration and meeting platforms that can help the co-workers meet and share ideas in a virtual setting.

The use of IT Support and IT services can also prove valuable in this regard. From time management to collaboration and feedback, there are plenty of tasks that can be automated and divided efficiently through the use of IT Services.

IT Support not only makes things easier for the employers and employees but also shares their workload so they can dedicate their attention and energies to working on more important things.

Being empathetic and offering support is also very important. Make sure that you stay in touch with your employees. Ask them how they feel and whether they need any type of help or support. Open all means of communication so your employees can always contact you and connect with you as needed.

Conclusion:

If you are wondering and worried about how to ensure business continuity when working on a remote basis, worry no more. The first thing you need to do is to prioritize the goals and objectives of your business and then list down a business continuity plan.

Take some time, analyze the needs of your business and the targets that you wish to accomplish in both the short term as well as the long term time period.

However, when working on a business continuity plan make sure that you don’t set rigid expectations. The plan should be adaptable and flexible enough to change as the situation demands.

‍

Name: Calculating The Cost Of IT Downtime For Industries
Introduction Paragraph: How much does IT downtime cost your industry? Discover the factors that influence the cost and the benefits of IT support in Los Angeles.
Post Body:
The Cost of IT Downtime

Take a minute to mull over what would happen if your business’ IT systems went down. If an essential link in your IT infrastructure – your servers, your Internet service, your line of business (LOB) application, or even your building’s electrical power – suddenly and unexpectedly became unavailable. Think about about the missed deadlines, the unhappy clients, the idle employees that you still have to pay. Then imagine the eventual loss of business if this became a frequent occurrence, or if the downtime occurred for an extended period of time. Think about how this could impact your future prospects if the downtime resulted in damage to your company’s reputation or branding. This is the cost of unplanned downtime.

An extreme example of IT downtime would be the Fastly outage that happened in June 2021. Fastly is a content delivery network that is designed to provide high availability and performance of internet content. Fastly fell victim to a cyberattack that took them, and some major websites on their network like Amazon and Reddit, offline. Service was restored a few hours later, but the damage was done. Fastly’s Q2 2021 Shareholder Letter reported lower revenue than expected and predicted deeper losses for the subsequent quarters. This report resulted in Fastly’s stock taking a 19% nosedive.

The cost of downtime by industry can vary, but for businesses that are not as equipped to bounce back, these ripple effects can do greater damage. For small businesses, the cost of unplanned downtime can even be fatal.

What is IT Downtime?

Downtime refers to a time period during which a service is unavailable. IT downtime means the duration for which a computer or a network is not accessible. But generally, the term IT Downtime is used when speaking of servers. Companies and industries aim to reduce the IT downtime as much as possible as it can not only be a cause of a lot of issues but can also drain the company’s finances.

There are several factors that can add up and cause an IT downtime. Here are some reasons why a company may have to experience an IT downtime.

1. Server Reboot:

Server Reboot is one of the most common reasons behind IT downtime. When the server is switched ON, it does not get started immediately and requires some time before it can respond to requests received. During this rebooting time, the server services are unavailable.

2. Immense Traffic:

Servers have a certain capacity of requests that they can tend to at a time. If the traffic exceeds this capacity, the server gives up and leads to an IT downtime. The average cost of network downtime as a result of immense traffic can add up to a huge sum.

3. Hardware/Software Failure:

Hardware and software failure is another reason why IT services may be unavailable for a certain time period. Depending upon the type and the extent of the failure, the IT downtime may vary from a couple of hours to even a few days if the damage is severe. Hence the system downtime impact in this case is unpredictable.

4. Power Failure:

Power failure is one of the most frequent causes behind IT downtime that can add to the cost of downtime 2020. Some small businesses have no backup power source therefore even the smallest power failure can cost a lot. But if the power failure lasts longer, even the businesses who have arranged for a backup power source end up suffering.

5. Unauthorized Attacks:

Hackers often attack servers and IT infrastructure and take over their services. When such unsolicited attacks lead to providing full control of the IT services to the attackers, the attackers can restrict access to the IT services and lead to IT downtime and failure.

Impact Of IT Downtime:

Now that we have discussed what is meant by the cost of network downtime, it is important to shed light over the impact of IT downtime on businesses. There are a number of different ways in which a business can be impacted by downtime and the unavailability of IT services. Here are some noticeable ways in which a business can be impacted by IT downtime:

1. Decrease In Productivity:

The modern era is the age of technology as a result of which businesses are heavily dependent on the internet to resume continuous services. From communication to other procedures, there are a lot of reasons why businesses require uninterrupted IT services.

IT downtime can disrupt the flow of such business activities and impact the productivity of a business. This cost of downtime by industry is especially fatal for companies and businesses who have transitioned towards cloud based IT services.

2. Loss Of Opportunities:

Almost all businesses have an online presence and make use of online channels to communicate and connect with their customers. When such businesses have to face IT downtime, a lot of business opportunities are lost. These lost opportunities surely add up to the cost of downtime small business has to endure.

The companies who face a lot of IT downtime also have to bear brand damage. Customers hate having to wait while the websites load properly. The longer users and visitors have to wait to avail IT services, the more frustrated they tend to get and it is likely for companies to lose clients and businesses when they face a lot of IT downtime.

3. Loss Of Data:

At times the server goes down or the IT services are interrupted in the middle of saving data. In this case, the IT downtime can lead to loss of data. Depending on the situation, sometimes, you might need to re-enter the data but in case of a severe IT downtime, the data may be lost entirely and it is likely that the data may or may not be recoverable.

Average Cost Of IT Downtime:

The average cost of IT downtime according to a study hosted by Gartner can be around $5600 per minute. However, this does not mean that the cost of the downtime is the same for every company or business.

One has to be aware of the fact that the cost of network downtime by industry can vary greatly. Some of the factors that can impact the average cost of IT downtime include the scope of the business and the time duration of the downtime.

The companies that are larger and can be labeled as enterprise scale businesses can face a larger cost of IT downtime. But in case of smaller industries and businesses, the average cost of the IT Downtime can be scaled down. However, it is important to mention that it is easier for large scale businesses to recover from an IT downtime even if the cost is more. Such businesses are established and can bounce back and recover from the damage easily.

But recovering from the IT downtime impacts and bearing the cost of IT downtime is difficult for small scale businesses. They do not have a lot of resources which leads to the IT downtime taking a huge toll on the smaller businesses.

The time period for which IT resources and services are unavailable also weighs in and adds to the cost of IT downtime. If the downtime is smaller, the recovery and the cost is obviously smaller but if the downtime lasts longer, the cost can also add up. Generally, the IT downtime cost is calculated by the hour. So depending on the number of hours for which the company faces the downtime, the cost can also vary and fluctuate.

Calculating IT Downtime Cost:

The general formula that serves as the cost of downtime calculator is:

Cost Of Downtime = Lost Revenue + Lost Productivity + Recovery Costs + Intangible Costs

To fully understand how to calculate IT downtime cost, it is important to dive in deeper and understand the factors that weigh in and add to the cost. Once you understand the related factors, calculating the IT downtime cost becomes a lot easier as all one needs to do is to sum up these factors and the cost will be

Lost Revenue:

Every business has to be aware of the revenue that is generated per hour. Since the IT downtime cost is calculated by the hour, therefore the revenue generated is also calculated per hour.

During uptime i.e. the time period during which the IT services are available and the business is working well, the company is making a certain amount of money per hour. This amount is known as the revenue per hour.

Since during the IT downtime, the company’s revenue is being lost, it is calculated in the negative and thus is known as lost revenue. A simple formula that can help you estimate the lost revenue is:

Lost Revenue = Revenue made per hour / Duration of downtime in hours x Uptime

Lost Productivity:

As discussed earlier, one aspect of the downtime cost definition is concerned with productivity. The fact that many businesses are highly dependent on the internet. Their operations take place online and therefore even a second of disconnection can affect the business operations.

During downtime, such operations require the internet and IT cannot be completed by the employers. This phenomenon is known as lost productivity. Employee productivity is not clearly tangible therefore it can be estimated only.

The metric used to calculate lost productivity is the employee salary. The employees are paid irrespective of the downtime therefore that sum of money accounts for the lost productivity. We will reinforce the fact that lost productivity is also calculated per hour just like all the other factors that add up to the cost of IT downtime. The lost productivity also adds up to the cost of unplanned downtime and it can be calculated as:

Lost Productivity = Salary of employees per hour x Percentage of Utilization x Total number of company employees.

Recovery Costs:

We dived into the details of the factors that lead to IT downtime. Other than the rebooting of the server, all other reasons that can lead to an IT downtime require some action to recover the server.

The recovery also comes with its own cost. Depending upon the reason for the IT downtime and the extent of the damage, the recovery cost can also vary greatly. If deeper damage has led to the IT downtime, it will require more efforts to recover and surely would come with a bigger recovery cost.

But in case of a shallow damage to the server hardware or even the software, the recovery cost will be minimal and the services can be resumed within a very affordable price range. This aspect of IT Downtime cost calculation cannot be predicted beforehand. It may vary greatly.

The only thing companies can do when it comes to recovery cost is to take measures that prevent such damage so the IT downtime can be avoided entirely. But when it comes to IT downtime recovery, don’t try to opt for a temporary solution. It may seem affordable at the moment, but it will not get to the root of the issue that led to the IT downtime and the problem may reoccur again.

In a nutshell, when it comes to recovery costs, they may seem like a burden at the time but this money spent can surely prevent you from facing another IT Downtime. Therefore, don’t be a miser and make the right choice so that IT downtime can be dealt with properly and its recurrence can be minimized.

Intangible Costs:

IT Downtime has a number of different impacts. Some damages can be calculated and added to the downtime cost, but there are other factors that are not clear. Some of these factors may not come with the cost straight up but the damage may add up with the passage of time.

For instance, the damage the IT downtime causes to the brand’s reputation or the lost opportunities that were caused by the IT downtime do not come with a straightforward cost but they do add to the gartner cost of downtime 2020. All these factors are known as the intangible costs.

Now that you have calculated all the factors individually, you can simply add up all the numbers and the final sum will be the cost of the IT downtime.

Minimizing Downtime Cost:

The goal for any company or business is to avoid the IT downtime entirely but this is not really possible. Technology is unreliable and there are a thousand things that can go wrong with it. Therefore, what companies can focus on is minimizing the downtime costs. Here are something that may be helpful in this regard:

  • Prepare A Disaster Recovery Plan - A disaster recovery plan is a step by step guide that can help a business recover from the IT downtime. It is the best way to respond to an IT downtime emergency. Therefore, make sure that your company has a disaster recovery plan in place.
  • Communication Is Key - Communication is extremely important. Around 35% of IT Downtime is caused by lack of proper communication. In a nutshell, make sure that you focus on effective communication at all costs.
  • Try Prevention - Going over the reasons behind the IT downtime, you can come up with several mechanisms that can help you prevent having to face an IT Downtime. It is forever true that “Prevention is better than cure”.
  • Analysis is Key - Many companies make the mistake of simply moving ahead after an IT Downtime. If you ever have to deal with an IT Downtime, make sure that you get to the root of the trouble and analyze the reason behind the IT downtime so that its recurrence can be prevented.

‍

Name: Can Your Mobile Phone Get Infected with Malware?
Introduction Paragraph: When using a mobile device, you’re holding a small computer in your hand, and it can be susceptible to malicious software just like your laptop or desktop.
Post Body:
Can your mobile phone be infected with malware? It sure can. You’re holding a small computer in your hand, and it can be susceptible to malicious software just like your laptop or desktop. Nuisanceware can be problematic when it is downloaded with other applications. Spyware can lead to stolen data, and one step up is stalkerware, which is often the most unethical and malicious of the spyware varieties.

How Does My Phone Get Malware?

If you notice that your mobile phone is getting unusual messages, has modified settings that you didn’t adjust, or is not running optimally, it may be infected.

App Downloads

Hackers spread malware via apps and downloads to your devices. An easy way to do that is via an app that you download. While official app stores usually ensure legitimacy, there are pirated versions that are easier to manipulate. They can appear legitimate but can contain spyware or other malware.

Phishing Emails

Using your phone to check email is convenient. However, beware of clicking on links that may be fraudulent. Phishing is one of the most widely used ways to attack an unsuspecting user.

Non-Secure WiFi

Be aware of automatic connections to wifi networks. Using a VPN can add an additional layer of protection when it comes to gaining access to your phone.

Text Messages or Voicemails

It can look legitimate, and it may sound legitimate, but do not give out personal information over the phone through a text or in response to a voice message request.

Protecting Your Device

Whether you’re protecting your personal or professional equipment, the same rules apply. Do not purchase a jailbroken device or modify your own equipment. This is especially true if it is issued by your employer for work. Always use a VPN instead of a public network. Download apps and software only from reputable sources. Often there are rules against any modifications if it is a work device. That includes downloads. Use encryption for all sensitive data. Ensure that you have multi-factor authentication enabled when possible.

And above all of the methods that you can engage with when it comes to cybersecurity, training is at the top of the list. Human error is the easiest entry point when it comes to illegally accessing your devices which can lead to network access.

If you believe that your device has been compromised with malware, notify your IT team immediately.

We hope the tips above help you feel safer and more confident about your mobile security. AllSafe IT’s comprehensive cybersecurity services are designed to identify, assess, and manage cybersecurity risks. We have aligned with the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) framework for the design of our cybersecurity solutions.

Name: Celebrating Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders in the Technology Industry
Introduction Paragraph: AllSafe IT highlights some notable AAPI individuals who have made significant strides in tech, paving the way for future generations.
Post Body:
May is a significant month as it marks Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month, providing us with a valuable opportunity to recognize and celebrate the achievements of Asian Americans and Pacific Islanders (AAPIs) across various fields. The technology industry, in particular, has witnessed remarkable contributions from AAPI professionals who have made substantial impacts on innovation, entrepreneurship, and the advancement of technology. In this blog post, we will highlight a few notable AAPI individuals who have made significant strides in the tech world, paving the way for future generations.

  1. Sundar Pichai: Hailing from Chennai, India, Sundar Pichai is the CEO of Alphabet Inc. and its subsidiary, Google. Pichai played a pivotal role in the development of various key Google products, including Chrome, Chrome OS, and Android. His visionary leadership has contributed to Google's exponential growth, enabling it to become a global technology powerhouse.
  2. Jen-Hsun Huang: Jen-Hsun Huang, born in Taiwan, is the co-founder and CEO of NVIDIA, a leading company in the field of graphics processing units (GPUs) and artificial intelligence (AI). Under Huang's guidance, NVIDIA has revolutionized the gaming and AI industries, playing a vital role in advancing computer graphics, deep learning, and autonomous vehicles.
  3. Arati Prabhakar: Arati Prabhakar, an Indian American technologist, served as the Director of the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA) from 2012 to 2017. Prabhakar played a crucial role in driving innovation and research, overseeing projects that explored groundbreaking technologies such as artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and advanced manufacturing.
  4. Jerry Yang: Jerry Yang, a Taiwanese American entrepreneur, co-founded Yahoo! along with David Filo in 1994. His contributions to the early development of the internet, search technology, and online advertising were instrumental in shaping the digital landscape. Yang's entrepreneurial spirit and innovative mindset continue to inspire countless individuals in the tech industry.
  5. Padmasree Warrior: Padmasree Warrior, born in India, is a renowned businesswoman and technology executive with extensive experience at top companies, including Cisco Systems and Motorola. She has played a significant role in driving technological advancements, particularly in the areas of cloud computing, cybersecurity, and digital transformation.
  6. Dado Banatao: Dado Banatao, a Filipino-American engineer and entrepreneur, has made significant contributions to the tech industry. He is widely known as the "Father of the PC Chipset" for his role in the development of early PC chipsets. Banatao has also been actively involved in supporting and mentoring aspiring entrepreneurs, especially in the Philippines.

During Asian American and Pacific Islander Heritage Month, it is important to acknowledge and celebrate the accomplishments of AAPI individuals in the technology industry. These are just a few examples of AAPI pioneers who have left an indelible mark on the tech world. Their contributions to innovation, leadership, and entrepreneurship continue to inspire and empower generations to come. By recognizing their achievements and highlighting their stories, we can foster a more inclusive tech industry that embraces diverse voices and perspectives. Let us celebrate the rich heritage and immense talent of AAPIs in the technology sector, both during this special month and throughout the year.
